<path>/home/cole/RustProjects/active/jfc/crates/jfc-voice/src/config.rs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
51: impl VadEngine {
52:     /// Resolve the configured engine from `JFC_VAD_ENGINE`.
53:     ///
54:     /// - An explicit value always wins: `neural`/`silero`/`onnx`/`ml` → Neural,
55:     ///   `energy`/`classic`/`default` → Energy.
56:     /// - When unset, the default depends on the build: if compiled with the
57:     ///   `vad-neural` feature the neural Silero engine is the default (it's the
58:     ///   more robust detector); otherwise Energy. This means a `vad-neural`
59:     ///   build is hands-free-neural out of the box, and `JFC_VAD_ENGINE=energy`
60:     ///   is the opt-out.
61:     pub fn from_env() -> Self {
62:         match std::env::var("JFC_VAD_ENGINE")
63:             .unwrap_or_default()
64:             .to_lowercase()
65:             .as_str()
66:         {
67:             "neural" | "silero" | "onnx" | "ml" => Self::Neural,
68:             "energy" | "classic" | "default" => Self::Energy,
69:             // Unset / unrecognized → build-dependent default.
70:             _ => Self::build_default(),
71:         }
72:     }
73: 
74:     /// The default engine for this build: Neural when the `vad-neural` feature
75:     /// is compiled in, Energy otherwise.
76:     pub const fn build_default() -> Self {
77:         #[cfg(feature = "vad-neural")]
78:         {
79:             Self::Neural
80:         }
81:         #[cfg(not(feature = "vad-neural"))]
82:         {
83:             Self::Energy
84:         }
85:     }
86: 
87:     /// Parse from a config-file string. `None` for unrecognized values.
88:     pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {
89:         match s.trim().to_lowercase().as_str() {
90:             "energy" | "classic" | "default" => Some(Self::Energy),
91:             "neural" | "silero" | "onnx" | "ml" => Some(Self::Neural),
92:             _ => None,
93:         }
94:     }
95: 
96:     pub fn label(self) -> &'static str {
97:         match self {
98:             Self::Energy => "energy",
99:             Self::Neural => "neural",
100:         }
101:     }
102: }
103: 
104: /// Resolved voice configuration.
105: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
106: pub struct VoiceConfig {
107:     /// Voice mode is enabled.
108:     pub enabled: bool,
109:     /// Hold or tap mode.
110:     pub mode: VoiceMode,
111:     /// Which VAD engine drives hands-free mode (energy vs neural Silero).
112:     pub vad_engine: VadEngine,
113:     /// Auto-submit after hold-to-talk release (hold mode only).
114:     pub auto_submit: bool,
115:     /// BCP-47 language code for STT (default "en").
116:     pub language: String,
117:     /// Which STT backend to prefer.
118:     pub backend: SttBackendKind,
119:     /// Override the Anthropic voice stream WebSocket URL.
120:     pub anthropic_voice_url: Option<String>,
121:     /// OpenAI API key for Whisper API backend.
122:     pub openai_api_key: Option<String>,
123:     /// Path to local whisper binary (e.g. "whisper-cpp", "whisper").
124:     pub local_whisper_bin: Option<String>,
125:     /// Path to whisper model file for local backend.
126:     pub local_whisper_model: Option<String>,
127:     /// Target-speaker gate: when enabled and a profile is enrolled, captured
128:     /// utterances that don't match the enrolled primary speaker (e.g. a movie /
129:     /// TV / another person in the room) are dropped instead of transcribed.
130:     /// OFF by default; opt-in via config `voice.speakerGate` or
131:     /// `JFC_VOICE_SPEAKER_GATE`.
132:     pub speaker_gate: bool,
133:     /// Path to the enrolled [`crate::speaker::SpeakerProfile`] JSON. When unset,
134:     /// defaults to `<config dir>/speaker_profile.json`. The gate no-ops when the
135:     /// file is missing/unreadable.
136:     pub speaker_profile_path: Option<String>,
137:     /// Optional override for the profile's calibrated acceptance threshold
138:     /// (`JFC_VOICE_SPEAKER_THRESHOLD`). Larger = more permissive.
139:     pub speaker_threshold: Option<f64>,
140:     /// Path to an ECAPA-TDNN/x-vector ONNX speaker-embedding model
141:     /// (`JFC_VOICE_SPEAKER_MODEL`). Only used when built with the
142:     /// `speaker-neural` feature; enables the SOTA-accuracy neural gate. When
143:     /// unset/unavailable the gate uses the classical MFCC-template score. The
144:     /// embedder reads this env directly; the field is here for discoverability
145:     /// and so the config can surface it.
146:     pub speaker_model_path: Option<String>,
147: }
148: 
149: /// Which STT backend to attempt first.
150: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
151: pub enum SttBackendKind {
152:     /// Try Anthropic WebSocket first, then OpenAI, then local.
153:     #[default]
154:     Auto,
155:     /// Anthropic real-time WebSocket (requires Claude.ai OAuth).
156:     Anthropic,
157:     /// OpenAI Whisper API (requires OPENAI_API_KEY).
158:     OpenAiWhisper,
159:     /// Local whisper.cpp binary (works offline).
160:     LocalWhisper,
161: }
162: 
163: impl VoiceConfig {
164:     /// Build from the `voice` serde_json::Value from ClaudeCompatibilityConfig.
165:     pub fn from_settings(voice_value: Option<&serde_json::Value>) -> Self {
166:         let mut cfg = Self {
167:             language: std::env::var("JFC_VOICE_LANGUAGE").unwrap_or_else(|_| "en".to_owned()),
168:             anthropic_voice_url: std::env::var("JFC_VOICE_ANTHROPIC_URL")
169:                 .ok()
170:                 .or_else(|| std::env::var("VOICE_STREAM_BASE_URL").ok()),

322:         // The engine comes from the env resolver, which uses the safe default

103: impl NeuralVad {
104:     /// Build the neural VAD. Returns an error if the ONNX session can't be
105:     /// created (e.g. the bundled model failed to load, or the ONNX Runtime
106:     /// shared library is missing under `load-dynamic`).
107:     pub fn new() -> anyhow::Result<Self> {
108:         if !crate::config::VadEngine::neural_runtime_enabled() {
109:             anyhow::bail!(
110:                 "neural VAD is disabled by default because ONNX Runtime can crash during native initialization; set JFC_VAD_ENABLE_NEURAL=1 to opt in"
111:             );
112:         }
113:         let detector = VoiceActivityDetector::builder()
114:             .sample_rate(SAMPLE_RATE)
115:             .chunk_size(CHUNK_SAMPLES)
116:             .build()
117:             .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("failed to build Silero VAD: {e}"))?;
118: 
119:         // Speech-probability thresholds mirror Silero's own canonical defaults:
120:         // `threshold = 0.5` to enter speech and `neg_threshold = threshold -
121:         // 0.15 = 0.35` to leave it (see silero-vad utils_vad.get_speech_timestamps).
122:         // The gap is the hysteresis that stops a brief mid-word confidence dip
123:         // from ending the utterance. Both overridable via env.
124:         let onset_threshold = env_f32("JFC_VAD_NEURAL_ONSET", 0.5).clamp(0.05, 0.95);
125:         let offset_threshold = env_f32("JFC_VAD_NEURAL_OFFSET", 0.35).clamp(0.01, onset_threshold);
126: 
127:         // Silence hangover before declaring end-of-utterance. Matches the
128:         // energy VAD's ~1s default (Deepgram utterance_end_ms), expressed in
129:         // 32ms chunks. Override via JFC_VAD_SILENCE_MS (shared with energy VAD).
130:         //
131:         // NOTE: this fixed tail-silence is intentionally generous. A short

36:     /// Choose the engine from config, falling back to the energy detector if

**Evaluating configuration issues**

I see there's an inconsistent state, with the config top being old while the tests are updated. It seems like the patch was only partially effective because the first update didn't apply. I should patch the top and consider adding comments again, though I wonder if we could reduce them while keeping only the necessary ones. I need to read the complete .cargo config output to check the linker settings and compare them with the JFC search output. Let's get that done.

Peony does have an explicit `.cargo/config.toml` forcing `/usr/bin/cc` and `linker-flavor=gcc`; JFC has no `.cargo` config in this repo. That makes the linker hypothesis plausible, but the immediate JFC bug remains that neural/ORT was being constructed by default.

38: # Logging
39: tracing = "0.1"
40: tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
41: # CLI (driver only)
42: clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
43: 
44: [profile.dev]
45: debug = "full"
46: split-debuginfo = "off"
47: strip = "none"
48: 
49: [profile.release]
50: debug = "full"
51: split-debuginfo = "off"
52: strip = "none"
53: 
54: [profile.unstripped]
55: inherits = "release"
56: debug = "full"
57: split-debuginfo = "off"
58: strip = "none"

[Project README: /home/cole/RustProjects/active/peony/README.md]
# peony

An experimental ELF linker written in Rust, targeting x86-64 Linux. Peony is
designed to be fast, incremental, and drop-in compatible with the standard
`ld`/`cc` command line so it can be used directly as the linker for `rustc` and
`gcc`-based toolchains.

## Features

- **ELF x86-64** — produces `ET_EXEC`, `ET_DYN` (PIE), and shared-object
  (`-shared`) outputs
- **Shared libraries** — full `ET_DYN` shared object support: `.dynsym`,
  `.plt`, `.got.plt`, `DT_SONAME`, `--version-script` for cdylib exports
- **TLS** — General-Dynamic, Local-Dynamic, and Initial-Exec TLS models for
  both executables and shared objects
- **GOT / PLT** — synthesised `.got`, `.got.plt`, `.plt`, and `.plt.got`
  sections; IFUNC (`R_X86_64_IRELATIVE`) support
- **Dynamic relocations** — `R_X86_64_RELATIVE`, `GLOB_DAT`, `JUMP_SLOT`,
  `DTPMOD64 / DTPOFF64 / TPOFF64`
- **`--gc-sections`** — mark-and-sweep dead-section elimination rooted at the
  entry point and (for shared objects) all exported symbols
- **COMDAT deduplication** — eliminates duplicate C++ inline/template sections
  across translation units
- **Incremental linking** (`--incremental`) — fingerprint-based cache that
  skips re-linking when inputs are unchanged
- **Linker-synthesised symbols** — `_end`, `_edata`, `__bss_start`,
  `_GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_`, `__executable_start`, `__dso_handle`, etc.
- **`--defsym`** — define absolute symbols on the command line
- **`--build-id`** — emit a `.note.gnu.build-id` section
- **Parallel** — work-stealing thread pool ([ws-deque]) for parallel section
  scanning and layout
- **`ld`-compatible CLI** — unknown flags are silently ignored so `cc`/`rustc`
  can invoke peony as a drop-in replacement

[ws-deque]: https://github.com/coleleavitt/ws-deque

## Crate layout

| Crate | Role |
|---|---|
| `peony` | Driver binary — CLI parsing, top-level link pipeline |
| `peony-object` | ELF parsing: `InputObject`, `InputSection`, `InputSymbol`, shared-object metadata |
| `peony-symbols` | Global symbol table — resolution, COMDAT, weak/import handling |
| `peony-layout` | Section/segment layout, address assignment, GC, TLS block placement |
| `peony-reloc` | Relocation scanning and application (static + dynamic) |
| `peony-emit` | ELF binary serialisation to disk |
| `peony-cache` | Incremental cache — fingerprinting, manifest read/write |

## Building

```sh
cargo build --release
```

Requires a recent nightly or stable Rust (1.73+).

## Usage

Peony is invoked like `ld`:

```sh
peony -o output input.o [input2.o ...] [-L dir] [-l lib] [flags]
```

### Key flags

| Flag | Description |
|---|---|
| `-o FILE` | Output file (default `a.out`) |
| `-e SYM` | Entry symbol (default `_start`) |
| `-L DIR` | Add library search directory |
| `-l NAME` | Link library `libNAME.{a,so}` |
| `--gc-sections` | Dead-strip unreachable sections |
| `--incremental` | Enable incremental link cache |
| `--build-id` | Emit `.note.gnu.build-id` |
| `-pie` / `-no-pie` | Position-independent executable |
| `-shared` | Produce a shared object (`ET_DYN`) |
| `-soname NAME` | Set `DT_SONAME` |
| `--version-script FILE` | Export/localise symbols per version script |
| `--defsym SYM=VAL` | Define an absolute symbol |
| `--threads N` | Worker thread count (0 = auto) |
| `-s` / `--strip-all` | Strip symbol table and debug sections |
| `-S` / `--strip-debug` | Strip debug sections but keep `.symtab` |

### Invoking from rustc

```sh
RUSTFLAGS="-C linker=/path/to/peony" cargo build
```

Or set `linker = "/path/to/peony"` in `.cargo/config.toml`.

## Testing

The test suite links real object files and compares output against reference
linkers (mold corpus, real-C objects, shared-library tests, TLS, relocations):

```sh
cargo test
```

## Profiling & tracing (`peony-prof`)

peony has a built-in profiler so you measure where a link spends its time —
and follow a bug through the pipeline — from *inside* the linker, instead of
guessing with external `strace`/`perf`.

```sh
peony --stats <args>    # phase breakdown table: parse/resolve/scan/layout/emit
peony --trace <args>    # call-flow tree: caller→callee by file:line, + events
```

`--stats` prints each phase's wall-clock, %, span count, byte/item throughput,
hot-path counters, and RSS snapshots with deltas. `--trace` additionally records the nested call flow with
source locations and point events (e.g. `archive-round: round 2: checked 3,
skipped 1, parsed 1, pulled 7, 3 undef left`), so you can see *what happened per
line* — this is how the O(N²) archive fixpoint was found and fixed. `--trace-stack`
adds Rust backtraces to each trace frame/event for deep bug hunts where function
stack and instruction-address context matter. All modes are near-zero cost when off
(a single atomic load short-circuits).

## Benchmarking

peony links real C, C++ (iostream/exceptions/STL), and Rust programs correctly,
and is benchmarked against mold, lld, and GNU ld with a correctness-gated
harness (a fast *wrong* linker is excluded from timing). See
[`bench/BENCHMARKING.md`](bench/BENCHMARKING.md) for methodology and the honest
baseline table.

```sh
bench/capture.sh rust-bin rust-hello /path/to/cargo/project --release
bench/bench.sh --runs 20 --warmup 5
```

Micro-benchmarks of the internal hot paths run under criterion / CodSpeed:

```sh
cargo bench -p peony-bench
```

## Formal verification

`rocq-tests/` holds nine machine-checked Rocq/Coq proofs (zero axioms beyond
functional extensionality). They cover GC reachability, layout congruence,
relocation disjoint-write determinism, symbol-resolution semilattice, and three
results that justify beating a from-scratch linker on the edit–rebuild loop:
incremental-relink soundness + the O(affected) cost bound, parallel-schedule
work–span optimality, and ICF (identical code folding) soundness. `make` in
`rocq-tests/` is the pass/fail oracle.

## License

Licensed under either of [Apache 2.0](LICENSE-APACHE) or [MIT](LICENSE-MIT) at
your option.

318:     fn tone_chunk(freq_hz: f64, amplitude: i16) -> Vec<u8> {
319:         (0..CHUNK_SAMPLES)
320:             .flat_map(|i| {
321:                 let t = i as f64 / SAMPLE_RATE as f64;
322:                 let v = (amplitude as f64 * (std::f64::consts::TAU * freq_hz * t).sin()) as i16;
323:                 v.to_le_bytes()

389:     /// Drive the state machine with a synthetic high-confidence probability,
390:     /// independent of model output, so onset/endpoint logic is deterministic.
391:     fn voiced_prob(vad: &mut NeuralVad) -> Option<VadEvent> {
392:         vad.process_probability(0.95)
393:     }
394:     fn silent_prob(vad: &mut NeuralVad) -> Option<VadEvent> {
395:         vad.process_probability(0.01)
396:     }
397: 
398:     // REGRESSION (long-idle recurrent-state drift): while idle, the Silero LSTM
399:     // state must be reset after a sustained run of non-speech so a long quiet
400:     // stretch can't bias the next onset ("go quiet and it stops detecting").
401:     // We assert the idle silence counter trips the reset cadence and zeroes.
402:     #[test]
403:     fn idle_reset_fires_after_sustained_silence_robust() {
404:         let mut vad = NeuralVad::new().expect("Silero model should load");
405:         vad.idle_reset_chunks = 5; // small cadence for the test
406:         // Feed silence chunks up to just before the reset boundary.
407:         for _ in 0..4 {
408:             assert_eq!(silent_prob(&mut vad), None);
409:         }
410:         assert_eq!(vad.idle_silent_chunks, 4, "should accumulate idle silence");
411:         // The 5th non-speech chunk hits the cadence and resets the counter.
412:         assert_eq!(silent_prob(&mut vad), None);
413:         assert_eq!(
414:             vad.idle_silent_chunks, 0,
415:             "idle reset must zero the silence counter at the cadence"
416:         );
417:         assert!(!vad.is_speaking(), "idle reset must not enter speech");
418:     }
419: 
420:     // The idle reset is gated to the idle state: once we're mid-utterance the
421:     // model state must NOT be reset on within-utterance pauses (that would wipe
422:     // the recurrent context mid-word).
423:     #[test]
424:     fn idle_reset_does_not_fire_during_speech_robust() {
425:         let mut vad = NeuralVad::new().expect("Silero model should load");
426:         vad.idle_reset_chunks = 3;
427:         vad.min_speech_chunks = 1;

430:             voiced_prob(&mut vad);
431:         }
432:         assert!(vad.is_speaking());
433:         // A within-utterance pause shorter than the hangover: idle_silent_chunks
434:         // must stay at zero because we're in_speech, not idle.
435:         for _ in 0..2 {
436:             silent_prob(&mut vad);
437:         }
438:         assert_eq!(
439:             vad.idle_silent_chunks, 0,
440:             "idle reset counter must not advance while in speech"
441:         );
442:     }
443: 
444:     // The idle reset cadence can be disabled (idle_reset_chunks == 0).
445:     #[test]
446:     fn idle_reset_disabled_when_zero_normal() {
447:         let mut vad = NeuralVad::new().expect("Silero model should load");
448:         vad.idle_reset_chunks = 0;

451:         }
452:         assert_eq!(

454:             "with reset disabled, the idle counter must never advance"
455:         );
456:     }
457: 
458:     // REGRESSION (anti-truncation): a stray low-probability chunk immediately
459:     // after onset must not fire SpeechEnd before the minimum utterance length,
460:     // so the start of a sentence can't be truncated — parity with the energy VAD.
461:     #[test]
462:     fn min_speech_guard_blocks_immediate_end_robust() {
463:         let mut vad = NeuralVad::new().expect("Silero model should load");
464:         vad.silence_chunks = 2; // easy to end if unguarded
465:         vad.min_speech_chunks = 10; // ~320ms guard
466:         // Just enough voiced chunks to start speech.
467:         for _ in 0..vad.onset_chunks {
468:             voiced_prob(&mut vad);
469:         }
470:         assert!(vad.is_speaking());
471:         // Immediate silence — without the guard this ends after 2 chunks.
472:         let ends = (0..5)
473:             .filter_map(|_| silent_prob(&mut vad))
474:             .filter(|e| *e == VadEvent::SpeechEnd)
475:             .count();
476:         assert_eq!(
477:             ends, 0,
478:             "min-speech guard must block a truncating SpeechEnd"
479:         );
480:     }
481: 
482:     // A genuine end-of-utterance (past the hangover, after enough speech) still
483:     // fires exactly one SpeechEnd — we didn't break endpointing.
484:     #[test]
485:     fn real_end_still_fires_after_hangover_normal() {
486:         let mut vad = NeuralVad::new().expect("Silero model should load");
487:         vad.silence_chunks = 3;
488:         vad.min_speech_chunks = 2;
489:         for _ in 0..vad.onset_chunks {
490:             voiced_prob(&mut vad);
491:         }
492:         // A few more voiced chunks to clear the min-speech guard.
493:         for _ in 0..3 {
494:             voiced_prob(&mut vad);
495:         }
496:         assert!(vad.is_speaking());
497:         let ends = (0..6)
498:             .filter_map(|_| silent_prob(&mut vad))
499:             .filter(|e| *e == VadEvent::SpeechEnd)
500:             .count();
501:         assert_eq!(
502:             ends, 1,
503:             "a real end-of-utterance must fire exactly one SpeechEnd"
504:         );
505:         assert!(!vad.is_speaking());
506:     }
507: 
508:     // REGRESSION (breath/stanza pause): a within-utterance pause shorter than
509:     // the hangover must NOT end the turn — the user pausing for breath or a
510:     // mid-thought "stanza" keeps one segment.
511:     #[test]
512:     fn within_utterance_pause_stays_one_segment_robust() {
513:         let mut vad = NeuralVad::new().expect("Silero model should load");
514:         vad.silence_chunks = 30; // ~1s hangover
515:         vad.min_speech_chunks = 2;
516:         for _ in 0..vad.onset_chunks {
517:             voiced_prob(&mut vad);
518:         }
519:         assert!(vad.is_speaking());
520:         // Several short breath pauses, each well under the hangover, interleaved
521:         // with speech — must produce zero SpeechEnd events.
522:         let mut ends = 0;
523:         for _ in 0..6 {
524:             for _ in 0..5 {
525:                 ends += voiced_prob(&mut vad)
526:                     .iter()
527:                     .filter(|e| **e == VadEvent::SpeechEnd)
528:                     .count();
529:             }
530:             for _ in 0..10 {
531:                 ends += silent_prob(&mut vad)
532:                     .iter()
533:                     .filter(|e| **e == VadEvent::SpeechEnd)
534:                     .count();
535:             }
536:         }
537:         assert_eq!(
538:             ends, 0,
539:             "short within-utterance pauses must not fire SpeechEnd"
540:         );
541:         assert!(
542:             vad.is_speaking(),
543:             "should still be mid-utterance after breath pauses"
544:         );
545:     }

51: impl VadEngine {
52:     /// Resolve the configured engine from `JFC_VAD_ENGINE`.
53:     ///
54:     /// - `energy`/`classic`/`default` → Energy.
55:     /// - `neural`/`silero`/`onnx`/`ml` → Neural only when
56:     ///   `JFC_VAD_ENABLE_NEURAL=1` is also set.
57:     /// - When unset, or when neural is requested without the unsafe opt-in, use
58:     ///   Energy. ONNX Runtime can segfault during environment construction on
59:     ///   some hosts, before Rust can return an error.
60:     pub fn from_env() -> Self {
61:         match std::env::var("JFC_VAD_ENGINE")
62:             .unwrap_or_default()
63:             .to_lowercase()
64:             .as_str()
65:         {
66:             "neural" | "silero" | "onnx" | "ml" if Self::neural_runtime_enabled() => {
67:                 Self::Neural
68:             }
69:             "energy" | "classic" | "default" => Self::Energy,
70:             // Unset / unrecognized / neural without opt-in → safe default.
71:             _ => Self::build_default(),
72:         }
73:     }
74: 
75:     /// The safe default engine for every build.
76:     pub const fn build_default() -> Self {
77:         Self::Energy
78:     }
79: 
80:     /// Whether this process is allowed to construct the native neural VAD.
81:     pub fn neural_runtime_enabled() -> bool {
82:         env_flag("JFC_VAD_ENABLE_NEURAL")
83:     }
84: 
85:     /// Parse from a config-file string. `None` for unrecognized values.
86:     pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {
87:         match s.trim().to_lowercase().as_str() {
88:             "energy" | "classic" | "default" => Some(Self::Energy),
89:             "neural" | "silero" | "onnx" | "ml" => Some(Self::Neural),
90:             _ => None,
91:         }
92:     }
93: 
94:     pub fn label(self) -> &'static str {
95:         match self {
96:             Self::Energy => "energy",
97:             Self::Neural => "neural",
98:         }
99:     }
100: }
101: 
102: /// Resolved voice configuration.
103: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
104: pub struct VoiceConfig {
105:     /// Voice mode is enabled.
106:     pub enabled: bool,
107:     /// Hold or tap mode.
108:     pub mode: VoiceMode,
109:     /// Which VAD engine drives hands-free mode (energy vs neural Silero).
110:     pub vad_engine: VadEngine,
111:     /// Auto-submit after hold-to-talk release (hold mode only).
112:     pub auto_submit: bool,
113:     /// BCP-47 language code for STT (default "en").
114:     pub language: String,
115:     /// Which STT backend to prefer.
116:     pub backend: SttBackendKind,
117:     /// Override the Anthropic voice stream WebSocket URL.
118:     pub anthropic_voice_url: Option<String>,
119:     /// OpenAI API key for Whisper API backend.
120:     pub openai_api_key: Option<String>,
121:     /// Path to local whisper binary (e.g. "whisper-cpp", "whisper").
122:     pub local_whisper_bin: Option<String>,
123:     /// Path to whisper model file for local backend.
124:     pub local_whisper_model: Option<String>,
125:     /// Target-speaker gate: when enabled and a profile is enrolled, captured
126:     /// utterances that don't match the enrolled primary speaker (e.g. a movie /
127:     /// TV / another person in the room) are dropped instead of transcribed.
128:     /// OFF by default; opt-in via config `voice.speakerGate` or
129:     /// `JFC_VOICE_SPEAKER_GATE`.
130:     pub speaker_gate: bool,
131:     /// Path to the enrolled [`crate::speaker::SpeakerProfile`] JSON. When unset,
132:     /// defaults to `<config dir>/speaker_profile.json`. The gate no-ops when the
133:     /// file is missing/unreadable.
134:     pub speaker_profile_path: Option<String>,
135:     /// Optional override for the profile's calibrated acceptance threshold
136:     /// (`JFC_VOICE_SPEAKER_THRESHOLD`). Larger = more permissive.
137:     pub speaker_threshold: Option<f64>,
138:     /// Path to an ECAPA-TDNN/x-vector ONNX speaker-embedding model
139:     /// (`JFC_VOICE_SPEAKER_MODEL`). Only used when built with the
140:     /// `speaker-neural` feature; enables the SOTA-accuracy neural gate. When
141:     /// unset/unavailable the gate uses the classical MFCC-template score. The
142:     /// embedder reads this env directly; the field is here for discoverability
143:     /// and so the config can surface it.
144:     pub speaker_model_path: Option<String>,
145: }
146: 
147: /// Which STT backend to attempt first.
148: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
149: pub enum SttBackendKind {
150:     /// Try Anthropic WebSocket first, then OpenAI, then local.
151:     #[default]
152:     Auto,
153:     /// Anthropic real-time WebSocket (requires Claude.ai OAuth).
154:     Anthropic,
155:     /// OpenAI Whisper API (requires OPENAI_API_KEY).
156:     OpenAiWhisper,
157:     /// Local whisper.cpp binary (works offline).
158:     LocalWhisper,
159: }
160: 
161: impl VoiceConfig {
162:     /// Build from the `voice` serde_json::Value from ClaudeCompatibilityConfig.
163:     pub fn from_settings(voice_value: Option<&serde_json::Value>) -> Self {
164:         let mut cfg = Self {
165:             language: std::env::var("JFC_VOICE_LANGUAGE").unwrap_or_else(|_| "en".to_owned()),

117: default = ["hooks", "permission-automation", "anthropic-oauth-sensitive", "intent-gate", "vad-neural"]
118: # Compile the neural (Silero) VAD backend into the binary, but keep energy VAD
119: # as the safe runtime default. Neural construction crosses ONNX Runtime FFI and
120: # can segfault before Rust receives an error on some hosts, so it requires the

_Thinking:_

**Cleaning up artifacts**

I’m considering what to remove before finalizing my work. Should I remove the .git/info/exclude line? The .debug-journal artifact isn’t really part of the source, so I need to clean that up. But maybe I should mention in the final that the debug journal was removed. I also need to focus on eliminating local artifacts, but it seems the modification in .git/info/exclude isn't being tracked according to my git status.

_Thinking:_

**Evaluating project updates**

I’m determining if I need to remove the .debug-journal and whether I should summarize my findings before that. It doesn't seem necessary to keep it, but if I remove it, I should apply a patch for .git/info/exclude. I won’t remove it right now, though, until I'm finished. I'm also considering updating my memory since this is a durable project. Regarding Peony, it’s not the immediate cause of issues, but I need to further investigate.

_Thinking:_

**Investigating debugging issues**

I’m trying to figure out why GDB/NM can’t parse certain files, possibly due to Peony. It’s strange that JFC doesn’t have a .cargo config, but GDB says “file format not recognized,” while readelf works fine. Maybe it’s because GDB uses libbfd and encounters unsupported section headers or compressed DWARF. There’s also an issue with LLDB loading it but showing debug invalid abbreviation. I’ll need to investigate more on the linker and check the global cargo configuration to see if Peony is involved.

_Thinking:_

**Exploring cargo options**

I’m considering using the cargo command with the `-Z` flag. I think I might run `cargo rustc -p jfc-voice --features vad-neural -- --print link-args`. This might help with compiling, though I'm not entirely sure it will work. I also want to check the configurations in both `/.cargo/config.toml` and `.config/cargo` to see if there are any settings that could impact this process. There's definitely more to explore here!

The isolated tests now pass on the safe path. I’m checking the actual linker configuration next, including global Cargo config, because JFC itself has no local `.cargo/config.toml` while Peony does.

_Thinking:_



Global Cargo config is important here: your `~/.cargo/config.toml` sets `/home/cole/.cargo/bin/peony` as the default linker for x86_64. Peony itself overrides back to `/usr/bin/cc`, so I’m doing one controlled check with `/usr/bin/cc` to see if ORT still crashes when neural is explicitly enabled.

**Tool: bash**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "command": "env -u JFC_VAD_ENGINE JFC_VAD_ENABLE_NEURAL=1 cargo --config 'target.x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.linker=\"/usr/bin/cc\"' --config 'target.x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.rustflags=[\"-C\",\"linker-flavor=gcc\",\"-C\",\"link-self-contained=no\",\"-C\",\"target-cpu=native\"]' test -p jfc-voice --features vad-neural neural_vad::tests::silence_never_triggers_speech_normal -- --exact",

_Thinking:_

**Investigating linker issues**

I need to continue working on the user's request about using the peony linker correctly. It’s important to understand the current state since there are many tasks at hand. The global cargo already uses peony, but JFC lacks a local .cargo config. The user wants to ensure everything works properly. I've noticed a potential cause of the segfault linked with peony might be a null function pointer issue. It's clear I need to inspect peony to find a solution.

_Thinking:_

**Analyzing linker issues**

I need to use Rust programming practices and possibly load debugging while checking the git master branch. It’s vital to analyze the Peony linker issue and read the relevant Peony code since the user mentioned it. There seems to be a problem with handling relocation, leading to a null function pointer. I should identify missing relocations, especially relating to ONNX Runtime. I’ll compare ELF/dynamic relocations between binaries linked with Peony and cc, which might reveal mishandling of .init_array order. This is crucial for identifying the source of the null pointer issue.

_Thinking:_

**Inspecting Peony issues**

It seems that Peony might be failing to execute static constructors from libstdc++, even though the program does run many constructors when using the controlled cc binary. I need to focus on the Peony project, inspecting the .cargo config and the code to understand the problem better. This is growing into a larger issue, so I should update my to-do list and consider whether a refactor is necessary. Creating a code graph for Peony might also provide valuable insights.

I agree: the controlled test says “linked with Peony” crashes in ORT, while the same neural VAD test linked through `/usr/bin/cc` passes. I’m switching the active root-cause fix to Peony’s ELF linking behavior, not just papering over it in JFC.

```rust
127	
128	pub use peony_layout::TlsRef;
129	
130	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
131	pub enum SyntheticSlot {
132	    Got(SymbolId),
133	    Plt(SymbolId),
134	    /// General-Dynamic TLS GOT *pair* (DTPMOD64 + DTPOFF) for a TLS symbol. Only
135	    /// allocated when producing a shared object (an executable relaxes GD→LE).
136	    TlsGd(TlsRef),
137	    /// Initial-Exec TLS GOT slot (GOTTPOFF → TPOFF64) for a TLS symbol, in a `.so`.
138	    TlsIe(TlsRef),
139	    /// The module's single Local-Dynamic (LDM) TLS GOT pair (DTPMOD64 + 0).
140	    TlsLdm,
141	    /// TLSDESC GOT pair for GNU2 TLS descriptors in a shared object.
142	    TlsDesc(TlsRef),
143	}
144	
145	/// Result of the relocation scan: the GOT/PLT slots required, in stable order.
146	pub struct RelocScanResult {
147	    pub slots: Vec<SyntheticSlot>,
148	    pub slot_set: FxHashMap<SyntheticSlot, u64>,
149	}
150	
151	impl RelocScanResult {
152	    pub fn new() -> Self {
153	        Self {
154	            slots: Vec::new(),
155	            slot_set: FxHashMap::default(),
156	        }
157	    }
158	
159	    fn add(&mut self, slot: SyntheticSlot) {
160	        use std::collections::hash_map::Entry;
161	        if let Entry::Vacant(v) = self.slot_set.entry(slot) {
162	            v.insert(0);
163	            self.slots.push(slot);
164	        }
165	    }
166	
167	    /// The symbols needing a GOT slot, in slot order.
168	    pub fn got_symbols(&self) -> Vec<SymbolId> {
169	        self.slots
170	            .iter()
171	            .filter_map(|s| match s {
172	                SyntheticSlot::Got(id) => Some(*id),
173	                _ => None,
174	            })
175	            .collect()
176	    }
177	
178	    /// The symbols needing a PLT entry (imported functions called via `@PLT`).
179	    pub fn plt_symbols(&self) -> Vec<SymbolId> {
180	        self.slots
181	            .iter()
182	            .filter_map(|s| match s {
183	                SyntheticSlot::Plt(id) => Some(*id),
184	                _ => None,
185	            })
186	            .collect()
187	    }
188	
189	    /// TLS refs needing a General-Dynamic GOT pair, in slot order (shared).
190	    pub fn tls_gd_refs(&self) -> Vec<TlsRef> {
191	        self.slots
192	            .iter()
193	            .filter_map(|s| match s {
194	                SyntheticSlot::TlsGd(r) => Some(*r),
195	                _ => None,
196	            })
197	            .collect()
198	    }
199	
200	    /// TLS refs needing an Initial-Exec GOT slot, in slot order (shared).
201	    pub fn tls_ie_refs(&self) -> Vec<TlsRef> {
202	        self.slots
203	            .iter()
204	            .filter_map(|s| match s {
205	                SyntheticSlot::TlsIe(r) => Some(*r),
206	                _ => None,
207	            })
208	            .collect()
209	    }
210	
211	    /// Whether the module needs a Local-Dynamic (LDM) TLS GOT pair (shared).
212	    pub fn needs_tls_ldm(&self) -> bool {
213	        self.slots
214	            .iter()
215	            .any(|s| matches!(s, SyntheticSlot::TlsLdm))
216	    }
217	
218	    /// TLS refs needing TLSDESC GOT pairs, in slot order.
219	    pub fn tls_desc_refs(&self) -> Vec<TlsRef> {
220	        self.slots
221	            .iter()
222	            .filter_map(|s| match s {
223	                SyntheticSlot::TlsDesc(r) => Some(*r),
224	                _ => None,
225	            })
226	            .collect()
227	    }
228	}
229	
230	impl Default for RelocScanResult {
231	    fn default() -> Self {
232	        Self::new()
233	    }
234	}
235

144	}
145	
146	/// Inputs needed to lay out a dynamic executable.
147	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
148	pub struct DynamicInfo {
149	    /// Imported symbol names, in `.dynsym` order (dynsym index = position + 1).
150	    pub imports: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
151	    /// Per-import version requirement, parallel to `imports`: the version string
152	    /// (e.g. `GLIBC_2.34`), or `None` for an unversioned import. Drives
153	    /// `.gnu.version` / `.gnu.version_r`.
154	    pub import_versions: Vec<Option<Vec<u8>>>,
155	    /// Per-import providing-library soname, parallel to `imports` (e.g.
156	    /// `libc.so.6`). Groups version requirements per-library in `.gnu.version_r`.
157	    pub import_sonames: Vec<Option<String>>,
158	    /// `DT_NEEDED` shared-library names.
159	    pub needed: Vec<String>,
160	    /// True when producing a position-independent executable (ET_DYN). PIE
161	    /// requires `R_X86_64_RELATIVE` dynamic relocations for every absolute
162	    /// pointer the loader must bias.
163	    pub pie: bool,
164	    /// Number of `R_X86_64_RELATIVE` relocations to reserve space for in
165	    /// `.rela.dyn` (computed by the driver via `peony_reloc::count_relative`).
166	    /// The actual entries are filled post-layout from
167	    /// `peony_reloc::collect_relative` and emitted by `peony-emit`.
168	    pub n_relative: usize,
169	    /// How many of `n_relative` are `R_X86_64_IRELATIVE` (IFUNC). Used to gate
170	    /// `DT_RELACOUNT`, which counts only the leading plain-RELATIVE entries:
171	    /// when `n_relative == n_irelative` (IFUNC-only), no DT_RELACOUNT is emitted.
172	    pub n_irelative: usize,
173	    /// Producing a shared object (`-shared`): emit exported defined globals into
174	    /// `.dynsym` (with a real SysV `.hash`), set `DT_SONAME`, and omit
175	    /// `PT_INTERP`/`.interp` (a `.so` has no program interpreter).
176	    pub shared: bool,
177	    /// `DT_SONAME` for a shared object (the library's logical name).
178	    pub soname: Option<String>,
179	    /// Exported symbols for a shared object's `.dynsym`, in export order:
180	    /// `(name, st_info, st_other, output_section_index_or_special, value, size)`.
181	    /// Filled by the driver after layout addresses are final is not possible
182	    /// (sizes drive layout), so names+metadata are provided here and the final
183	    /// `st_value`/`st_shndx` are resolved at emit time by name.
184	    pub exports: Vec<ExportSym>,
185	    /// Number of TLS dynamic relocations (`R_X86_64_DTPMOD64`/`DTPOFF64`/
186	    /// `TPOFF64`) the shared-object TLS GOT region needs, to size `.rela.dyn`.
187	    pub n_tls_reloc: usize,
188	    /// Imported DSO data symbols that require executable-owned copy storage and
189	    /// `R_X86_64_COPY` dynamic relocations.
190	    pub copy_relocs: Vec<SymbolId>,
191	    /// Number of symbolic `R_X86_64_64` dynamic relocations to reserve in
192	    /// `.rela.dyn` for `R64` data sites that reference an imported symbol (e.g.
193	    /// gcc's `.data.rel.local.DW.ref.*` EH slots). Filled post-layout from
194	    /// `peony_reloc::collect_symbolic_data_relocs`.
195	    pub n_symbolic_data: usize,
196	}
197

96	///
97	/// IDs are assigned in insertion order and are stable within a single link.

99	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
100	pub struct SymbolId(pub u32);
101	
102	/// Which input object defines this symbol (or where it was first seen).
103	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]

... (gap) ...

200	    /// unsized map repeatedly grows and rehashes (profiling a 423-object link
201	    /// showed `reserve_rehash` at ~1.7% of self-time). Reserving once removes
202	    /// that. Capacity is a hint only — correctness is identical to `new`.
203	    pub fn with_capacity(symbols: usize) -> Self {
204	        Self {
205	            resolutions: FxHashMap::with_capacity_and_hasher(symbols, Default::default()),
206	            names: Vec::with_capacity(symbols),
207	            object_paths: Vec::new(),
208	        }

#### peony/src/main.rs — section_records(function), Layout(references), SectionRecord(references)

```rust
787	/// without relaying out. Capacity currently equals size (no incremental padding
788	/// is reserved yet); a future padding pass can widen it. Reads the emitted
789	/// output once and slices each allocatable section by its file range.
790	fn section_records(
791	    output: &Path,
792	    layout: &peony_layout::Layout,
793	) -> Option<Vec<peony_cache::SectionRecord>> {
794	    let bytes = std::fs::read(output).ok()?;
795	    let mut records = Vec::new();
796	    for sec in &layout.output_sections {
797	        // Only file-backed allocatable sections have stable, patchable bytes.
798	        if sec.sh_type == peony_object::elf::SHT_NOBITS
799	            || sec.sh_flags & peony_object::elf::SHF_ALLOC == 0
800	        {
801	            continue;
802	        }
803	        let start = sec.sh_offset as usize;
804	        let end = start + sec.sh_size as usize;
805	        let fingerprint = match bytes.get(start..end) {
806	            Some(slice) => peony_cache::Fingerprint::of_bytes(slice),
807	            None => continue, // out-of-range (e.g. .tbss): skip
808	        };
809	        records.push(peony_cache::SectionRecord {
810	            name: sec.name.clone(),
811	            fingerprint,
812	            file_offset: sec.sh_offset,
813	            size: sec.sh_size,
814	            capacity: sec.sh_size, // no padding reserved yet
815	            virtual_address: sec.sh_addr,
816	        });
817	    }
818	    Some(records)
819	}
820	
821	// ── Loading + resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
822	
```

#### peony-object/src/arena.rs — SectionData(struct), InputArena(struct), new(method), push_mmap(method), push_owned(method), +12 more

```rust
1	use memmap2::Mmap;
2	use rustc_hash::FxHashSet;
3	
4	use crate::{InputObject, Name};
5	
6	#[derive(Default)]
7	pub struct InputArena {
8	    pub(crate) mmaps: Vec<Mmap>,
9	    pub(crate) owned: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
10	    names: FxHashSet<Name>,
11	}
12	
13	impl InputArena {
14	    pub fn new() -> Self {
15	        Self::default()
16	    }
17	
18	    pub fn push_mmap(&mut self, m: Mmap) -> u32 {
19	        let id = self.mmaps.len() as u32;
20	        self.mmaps.push(m);
21	        id
22	    }
23	
24	    pub fn push_owned(&mut self, v: Vec<u8>) -> u32 {
25	        let id = self.owned.len() as u32;
26	        self.owned.push(v);
27	        id
28	    }
29	
30	    pub fn merge_parsed_owned(&mut self, obj: &mut InputObject, local_owned: Vec<Vec<u8>>) {
31	        let base = self.owned.len() as u32;
32	        if !local_owned.is_empty() {
33	            for sec in &mut obj.sections {
34	                if let DataSrc::Owned(i) = sec.data.src {
35	                    sec.data.src = DataSrc::Owned(base + i);
36	                }
37	            }
38	            self.owned.extend(local_owned);
39	        }
40	        self.intern_object_names(obj);
41	    }
42	
43	    pub fn intern_name(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> Name {
44	        if let Some(name) = self.names.get(bytes) {
45	            return name.clone();
46	        }
47	        let name = Name::from_slice(bytes);
48	        self.names.insert(name.clone());
49	        name
50	    }
51	
52	    pub fn intern_object_names(&mut self, obj: &mut InputObject) {
53	        for section in &mut obj.sections {
54	            section.name = self.intern_name(section.name.as_bytes());
55	        }
56	        for symbol in &mut obj.symbols {
57	            if symbol.name.is_empty() || symbol.st_type == crate::elf::STT_SECTION {
58	                symbol.name = self.intern_name(symbol.name.as_bytes());
59	            }
60	        }
61	        for group in &mut obj.comdat_groups {
62	            group.signature = self.intern_name(group.signature.as_bytes());
63	        }
64	    }
65	
66	    pub fn interned_name_count(&self) -> usize {
67	        self.names.len()
68	    }
69	
70	    pub fn interned_name_bytes(&self) -> u64 {
71	        self.names.iter().map(|name| name.len() as u64).sum()
72	    }
73	
74	    pub fn intern_bytes(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> SectionData {
75	        assert!(
76	            bytes.len() <= u32::MAX as usize,
77	            "section too large for u32 len"
78	        );
79	        let len = bytes.len() as u32;
80	        let id = self.push_owned(bytes.to_vec());
81	        SectionData {
82	            src: DataSrc::Owned(id),
83	            off: 0,
84	            len,
85	        }
86	    }
87	
88	    #[inline]
89	    pub fn mmap_bytes(&self, file_id: u32) -> &[u8] {
90	        &self.mmaps[file_id as usize]
91	    }
92	
93	    #[inline]
94	    pub fn owned_bytes(&self, owned_id: u32) -> &[u8] {
95	        &self.owned[owned_id as usize]
96	    }
97	
98	    #[inline]
99	    pub fn bytes(&self, d: SectionData) -> &[u8] {
100	        let base: &[u8] = match d.src {
101	            DataSrc::Mmap(i) => &self.mmaps[i as usize],
102	            DataSrc::Owned(i) => &self.owned[i as usize],
103	        };
104	        &base[d.off as usize..d.off as usize + d.len as usize]
105	    }
106	}
107	
108	#[cfg(test)]
109	mod tests {
110	    use super::*;
111	
112	    #[test]
113	    fn intern_name_reuses_backing_storage() {
114	        let mut arena = InputArena::new();
115	        let first = arena.intern_name(b"same");
116	        let second = arena.intern_name(b"same");
117	        let third = arena.intern_name(b"different");
118	
119	        assert!(first.ptr_eq(&second));
120	        assert!(!first.ptr_eq(&third));
121	        assert_eq!(arena.interned_name_count(), 2);
122	        assert_eq!(arena.interned_name_bytes(), 13);
123	    }
124	}
125	
126	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
127	pub struct SectionData {
128	    pub src: DataSrc,
129	    pub off: u32,
130	    pub len: u32,
131	}
132	
133	impl SectionData {
134	    pub const EMPTY: SectionData = SectionData {
135	        src: DataSrc::Mmap(0),
136	        off: 0,
137	        len: 0,
138	    };
139	
140	    #[inline]
141	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
142	        self.len as usize
143	    }
144	
145	    #[inline]
146	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
147	        self.len == 0
148	    }
149	}
150	
151	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
152	pub enum DataSrc {
153	    Mmap(u32),
154	    Owned(u32),
155	}

The ELF comparison found two strong Peony clues: the Peony binary’s `PT_GNU_RELRO` only covers `.fini_array` instead of the whole RELRO region, and the crash frame sits in ONNX Runtime’s one-time environment init path. 791	/// Resolve a [`TlsRef`]'s offset within this module's TLS block, plus whether it
792	/// is an *imported* (undefined-here) TLS symbol. Returns `None` if it cannot be
793	/// resolved (e.g. dropped section).
794	fn tls_ref_offset(
795	    tref: TlsRef,
796	    objects: &[InputObject],
797	    symbols: &SymbolTable,
798	    layout: &Layout,
799	) -> Option<(u64, bool)> {
800	    match tref {
801	        TlsRef::Local(obj_id, sym_pos) => {
802	            let sym = objects.get(obj_id)?.symbols.get(sym_pos)?;
803	            let si = sym.section?;
804	            let base = layout.tls_offset(obj_id, si.0)?;
805	            Some((base + sym.value, false))
806	        }
807	        TlsRef::Global(id) => {
808	            let name = symbols.name_by_id(id)?;
809	            let res = symbols.lookup(name)?;
810	            match (res.defined_in, res.section_index) {
811	                (Some(def), Some(si)) => {
812	                    let base = layout.tls_offset(def.0 as usize, si)?;
813	                    Some((base + res.value, false))
814	                }
815	                // Imported / undefined-here TLS symbol: offset filled by loader.
816	                _ => Some((0, true)),
817	            }
818	        }
819	    }
820	}

## Assistant (Hephaestus - Deep Agent · GPT-5.5 · 16.4s)

_Thinking:_



This is now narrowed: the cc-linked binary has `R_X86_64_TPOFF64` dynamic relocations for libstdc++’s `_ZSt15__once_callable` and `_ZSt11__once_call`; the Peony-linked binary has no such relocations and turns those symbols into ABS zero. That explains `std::call_once` handing `pthread_once` a null callback.

```rust
1365	/// version node with a `global:` list of exact symbol names and a `local: *;`
1366	/// catch-all. `exact` holds the named globals; `global_wildcard` is true if
1367	/// `global: *` appears (export everything not explicitly localized).
1368	#[derive(Debug, Default)]
1369	struct VersionScript {
1370	    exact: std::collections::HashSet<Vec<u8>>,
1371	    global_wildcard: bool,
1372	}
1373	
1374	impl VersionScript {
1375	    /// Whether a symbol named `name` should be exported under this script.
1376	    fn exports(&self, name: &[u8]) -> bool {
1377	        self.global_wildcard || self.exact.contains(name)
1378	    }
1379	}
1380	
1381	/// Parse the subset of GNU version-script syntax rustc emits: `global:`/`local:`
1382	/// sections with `;`-terminated bare symbol names and `*` wildcards. Comments
1383	/// (`#`/`//`) and version-node names are ignored; we treat the file as one flat
1384	/// global/local partition (sufficient for cdylib export control).
1385	fn parse_version_script(path: &Path) -> Result<VersionScript> {
1386	    let text = std::fs::read_to_string(path)
1387	        .with_context(|| format!("reading version script `{}`", path.display()))?;
1388	    let mut vs = VersionScript::default();
1389	    let mut in_global = false;
1390	    for raw in text.lines() {
1391	        // Strip comments and braces/version-node tokens.
1392	        let line = raw.split('#').next().unwrap_or("");
1393	        let line = line.split("//").next().unwrap_or("");
1394	        for tok in line.split([' ', '\t', '{', '}', '(', ')']) {
1395	            let tok = tok.trim();
1396	            if tok.is_empty() {
1397	                continue;
1398	            }
1399	            match tok {
1400	                "global:" => in_global = true,
1401	                "local:" => in_global = false,
1402	                _ => {
1403	                    // A name list entry, possibly several `name;` on one line.
1404	                    for name in tok.split(';') {
1405	                        let name = name.trim();
1406	                        if name.is_empty() || name == ";" {
1407	                            continue;
1408	                        }
1409	                        if in_global {
1410	                            if name == "*" {
1411	                                vs.global_wildcard = true;
1412	                            } else {
1413	                                vs.exact.insert(name.as_bytes().to_vec());
1414	                            }
1415	                        }
1416	                    }
1417	                }
1418	            }
1419	        }
1420	    }
1421	    Ok(vs)
1422	}
1423	
1424	/// Expand any GNU linker-script inputs (e.g. the system `libc.so`, which is a
1425	/// `GROUP(...)` script) into the real object/library files they reference,
1426	/// recursively.
1427	fn expand_inputs(inputs: Vec<PathBuf>, search: &[PathBuf]) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>> {
1428	    use std::collections::VecDeque;
1429	    let mut out = Vec::new();
1430	    let mut work: VecDeque<PathBuf> = inputs.into();
1431	    while let Some(p) = work.pop_front() {
1432	        if peony_object::classify_file(&p) == peony_object::FileKind::Archive {
1433	            out.push(p);
1434	            continue;
1435	        }
1436	        if !is_linker_script(&p) {
1437	            out.push(p);
1438	            continue;
1439	        }
1440	        let dir = p.parent().map(Path::to_path_buf);
1441	        for r in parse_linker_script(&p)? {
1442	            match resolve_script_ref(&r, dir.as_deref(), search) {
1443	                Some(rp) => work.push_back(rp),
1444	                None => tracing::warn!("linker script `{}`: cannot resolve `{r}`", p.display()),
1445	            }
1446	        }
1447	    }
1448	    Ok(out)
1449	}
1450	
1451	/// A text file referencing GROUP/INPUT (and not an ELF/archive) is a linker script.
1452	///
```

473	pub fn count_dynamic_relocs(
474	    objects: &[InputObject],
475	    symbols: &SymbolTable,
476	    got_syms: &[SymbolId],
477	    tls: &peony_layout::TlsGotInfo,
478	    shared: bool,
479	) -> DynamicRelocCounts {
480	    let mut counts = DynamicRelocCounts::default();
481	    for obj in objects {
482	        for sec in &obj.sections {
483	            if sec.flags & peony_object::elf::SHF_ALLOC == 0 {
484	                continue;
485	            }
486	            for reloc in &sec.relocs {
487	                if reloc.r_type != r_x86_64::R64 {
488	                    continue;
489	                }
490	                let Some(sym) = obj
491	                    .symbols
492	                    .get(*obj.symbol_map.get(&reloc.symbol.0).unwrap_or(&usize::MAX))
493	                else {
494	                    continue;
495	                };
496	                if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
497	                    if sym.section.is_some() {
498	                        counts.relative_total += 1;
499	                        counts.irelative += usize::from(sym.is_ifunc);
500	                    }
501	                    continue;
502	                }
503	                let Some(res) = symbols.lookup(&sym.name) else {
504	                    continue;
505	                };
506	                if res.import && !res.copy_reloc {
507	                    counts.symbolic_data += 1;
508	                } else if res.is_defined() {
509	                    counts.relative_total += 1;
510	                    counts.irelative += usize::from(!res.import && res.is_ifunc);
511	                }
512	            }
513	        }
514	    }
515	
516	    for id in got_syms {
517	        let Some(res) = symbols
518	            .name_by_id(*id)
519	            .and_then(|name| symbols.lookup(name))
520	        else {
521	            continue;
522	        };
523	        if (!res.import || res.copy_reloc) && res.is_defined() {
524	            counts.relative_total += 1;
525	            counts.irelative += usize::from(!res.import && res.is_ifunc);
526	        }
527	    }
528	
529	    counts.tls = if shared {
530	        count_tls_relocs(objects, symbols, tls)
531	    } else {
532	        0
533	    };
534	    counts
535	}

1041	    /// Build the index from the objects and the frozen symbol table. Hashes each
1042	    /// object symbol's name once (≈49k total) — replacing the ≈216k per-reloc
1043	    /// name hashes with array indexing.
1044	    pub fn build(objects: &[InputObject], symbols: &'a SymbolTable) -> Self {
1045	        let by_object = objects
1046	            .iter()
1047	            .map(|obj| {
1048	                obj.symbols
1049	                    .iter()
1050	                    .map(|sym| {
1051	                        if sym.binding == Binding::Local || sym.name.is_empty() {
1052	                            None
1053	                        } else {
1054	                            symbols.lookup(&sym.name)
1055	                        }
1056	                    })
1057	                    .collect()
1058	            })
1059	            .collect();
1060	        SymIndex { by_object }
1061	    }
1062

1064	    /// symbol list) to its resolution, by integer indexing only.
1065	    #[inline]
1066	    pub fn resolution(&self, obj_id: usize, sym_pos: usize) -> Option<&'a SymbolResolution> {
1067	        *self.by_object.get(obj_id)?.get(sym_pos)?

466	    /// happen for a well-formed link) are `None`. Correctness is identical to
467	    /// repeated [`lookup`] calls — purely a lookup-cost optimization, gated by
468	    /// the byte-identical-output determinism tests.
469	    pub fn build_id_index(&self) -> Vec<Option<&SymbolResolution>> {
470	        let mut out: Vec<Option<&SymbolResolution>> = vec![None; self.names.len()];
471	        for name in &self.names {
472	            if let Some(r) = self.resolutions.get(name) {
473	                let idx = r.id.0 as usize;
474	                if idx < out.len() {
475	                    out[idx] = Some(r);
476	                }
477	            }
478	        }
479	        out
480	    }
481	
482	    /// Mutable lookup — used by layout to fill addresses.
483	    pub fn lookup_mut(&mut self, name: &[u8]) -> Option<&mut SymbolResolution> {
484	        self.resolutions.get_mut(name)
485	    }
486	
487	    /// Mark currently-undefined symbols that a shared library exports as dynamic
488	    /// imports (resolved at runtime). Returns how many references were satisfied.
489	    pub fn register_shared_exports(&mut self, exports: &[Vec<u8>]) -> usize {
490	        self.register_shared_exports_versioned(exports, &[], "")
491	    }
492	
493	    /// As [`register_shared_exports`], but also records each satisfied import's
494	    /// version requirement (parallel to `exports`; `versions` may be shorter, in
495	    /// which case missing entries are treated as unversioned) and the providing
496	    /// library's `soname` (for per-library `.gnu.version_r` grouping).
497	    pub fn register_shared_exports_versioned(
498	        &mut self,
499	        exports: &[Vec<u8>],
500	        versions: &[Option<Vec<u8>>],
501	        soname: &str,
502	    ) -> usize {
503	        let mut n = 0;
504	        for (i, name) in exports.iter().enumerate() {
505	            // An undefined reference satisfied by the shared library.
506	            let satisfy = matches!(
507	                self.resolutions.get(name.as_slice()),
508	                Some(r) if r.defined_in.is_none() && !r.import
509	            );
510	            if !satisfy {
511	                continue;
512	            }
513	            // Imports need a real SymbolId so they participate in GOT/.dynsym.
514	            let id = SymbolId(self.names.len() as u32);
515	            self.names.push(Name::from_slice(name));
516	            let ver = versions.get(i).cloned().flatten();
517	            let r = self.resolutions.get_mut(name.as_slice()).unwrap();
518	            r.import = true;
519	            r.copy_reloc = false;
520	            r.id = id;
521	            r.version = ver;
522	            r.soname = Some(soname.to_string());
523	            n += 1;
524	        }
525	        n
526	    }
527	
528	    /// Mark undefined references satisfied by shared-library export records, also
529	    /// preserving size/type metadata needed for `R_X86_64_COPY` decisions.
530	    pub fn register_shared_export_symbols(
531	        &mut self,
532	        exports: &[peony_object::SharedExport],
533	        soname: &str,
534	    ) -> usize {
535	        let mut n = 0;
536	        for ex in exports {
537	            let satisfy = matches!(
538	                self.resolutions.get(ex.name.as_slice()),
539	                Some(r) if r.defined_in.is_none() && !r.import
540	            );
541	            if !satisfy {
542	                continue;
543	            }
544	            let id = SymbolId(self.names.len() as u32);
545	            self.names.push(Name::from_slice(&ex.name));
546	            let r = self.resolutions.get_mut(ex.name.as_slice()).unwrap();
547	            r.import = true;
548	            r.copy_reloc = false;
549	            r.id = id;
550	            r.version = ex.version.clone();
551	            r.soname = Some(soname.to_string());
552	            r.size = ex.size;
553	            r.st_type = ex.st_type;
554	            n += 1;
555	        }
556	        n
557	    }
558	
559	    /// Mark an import as requiring an executable copy relocation.
560	    pub fn mark_copy_reloc(&mut self, name: &[u8]) {
561	        if let Some(r) = self.resolutions.get_mut(name) {
562	            if r.import {
563	                r.copy_reloc = true;
564	            }
565	        }
566	    }
567	
568	    /// Whether any symbol is a dynamic import (→ a dynamic executable is needed).
569	    pub fn has_imports(&self) -> bool {
570	        self.resolutions.values().any(|r| r.import)
571	    }
572	
573	    /// Ensure `name` has a real [`SymbolId`] (assigning one if it is still the
574	    /// placeholder `u32::MAX`). Used for weak-undefined symbols that are
575	    /// referenced through the GOT (e.g. `__gmon_start__`): they need a stable id
576	    /// so their GOT slot gets a recorded address (holding 0). Returns the id.
577	    pub fn ensure_id(&mut self, name: &[u8]) -> Option<SymbolId> {
578	        let needs = matches!(self.resolutions.get(name), Some(r) if r.id.0 == u32::MAX);
579	        if needs {
580	            let id = SymbolId(self.names.len() as u32);
581	            self.names.push(Name::from_slice(name));
582	            self.resolutions.get_mut(name).unwrap().id = id;
583	            Some(id)
584	        } else {
585	            self.resolutions.get(name).map(|r| r.id)
586	        }
587	    }
588	
589	    /// Define (or overwrite) `name` as an absolute symbol with `value`
590	    /// (used for `--defsym` and linker-provided symbols).
591	    pub fn define_absolute(&mut self, name: &[u8], value: u64) {
592	        let e = self
593	            .resolutions
594	            .entry(Name::from_slice(name))
595	            .or_insert_with(Self::make_undefined_resolution);
596	        e.defined_in = Some(ObjectId(0));
597	        e.section_index = None;
598	        e.value = value;
599	        e.virtual_address = value;
600	    }
601	
602	    /// The name bytes for a [`SymbolId`] (only valid for *defined* symbols).
603	    pub fn name_by_id(&self, id: SymbolId) -> Option<&[u8]> {
604	        self.names.get(id.0 as usize).map(Name::as_bytes)
605	    }
606	
607	    /// Iterate over all resolutions.
608	    pub fn iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = (&[u8], &SymbolResolution)> {
609	        self.resolutions.iter().map(|(k, v)| (k.as_bytes(), v))
610	    }
611	
612	    /// Mutable iteration over all resolutions (for address write-back).
613	    pub fn values_mut(&mut self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &mut SymbolResolution> {
614	        self.resolutions.values_mut()
615	    }
616	
617	    /// Number of distinct objects registered.
618	    pub fn object_count(&self) -> usize {
619	        self.object_paths.len()
620	    }
621	
622	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
623	        self.resolutions.len()
624	    }
625	
626	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
627	        self.resolutions.is_empty()
628	    }
629	}
630	
631	// ── Conflict resolution helper (free function to avoid borrow issues) ─────────
```

#### peony-object/src/shared.rs — SharedObject(struct), SharedExport(struct), parse_shared_object(function), Result(references), SharedObject(references), +2 more

```rust
6	use crate::{ObjectError, Result, elf};
7	
8	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
9	pub struct SharedObject {
10	    pub soname: String,
11	    pub exports: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
12	    pub export_versions: Vec<Option<Vec<u8>>>,
13	    pub export_symbols: Vec<SharedExport>,
14	}
15	
16	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
17	pub struct SharedExport {
18	    pub name: Vec<u8>,
19	    pub version: Option<Vec<u8>>,
20	    pub size: u64,
21	    pub st_type: u8,
22	}
23	
24	pub fn parse_shared_object(path: &Path) -> Result<SharedObject> {
25	    let data = std::fs::read(path).map_err(|e| ObjectError::Io {
26	        path: path.display().to_string(),
27	        source: e,
28	    })?;
29	    let elf: ElfFile64<Endianness> =
30	        ElfFile64::parse(data.as_slice()).map_err(|e| ObjectError::Parse {
31	            path: path.display().to_string(),
32	            source: e,
33	        })?;
34	
35	    let endian = elf.endian();
36	    let versions = elf
37	        .elf_section_table()
38	        .versions(endian, data.as_slice())
39	        .ok()
40	        .flatten();
41	
42	    let mut exports = Vec::new();
43	    let mut export_versions = Vec::new();
44	    let mut export_symbols = Vec::new();
45	    for sym in elf.dynamic_symbols() {
46	        if sym.is_undefined() || sym.is_local() {
47	            continue;
48	        }
49	        let Ok(name) = sym.name_bytes() else {
50	            continue;
51	        };
52	        if name.is_empty() {
53	            continue;
54	        }
55	        let ver = versions.as_ref().and_then(|vt| {
56	            let vidx = vt.version_index(endian, sym.index());
57	            match vt.version(vidx) {
58	                Ok(Some(v)) => Some(v.name().to_vec()),
59	                _ => None,
60	            }
61	        });
62	        let name = name.to_vec();
63	        let st_type = match sym.kind() {
64	            object::SymbolKind::Text => elf::STT_FUNC,
65	            object::SymbolKind::Data => elf::STT_OBJECT,
66	            object::SymbolKind::Tls => elf::STT_TLS,
67	            object::SymbolKind::Section => elf::STT_SECTION,
68	            object::SymbolKind::File => elf::STT_FILE,
69	            _ => elf::STT_NOTYPE,
70	        };
71	        export_symbols.push(SharedExport {
72	            name: name.clone(),
73	            version: ver.clone(),
74	            size: sym.size(),
75	            st_type,
76	        });
77	        exports.push(name);
78	        export_versions.push(ver);
79	    }
80	
81	    let soname = elf_soname(&elf, data.as_slice()).unwrap_or_else(|| {
82	        path.file_name()
83	            .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
84	            .unwrap_or_else(|| path.display().to_string())
85	    });
86	
87	    Ok(SharedObject {
88	        soname,
89	        exports,
90	        export_versions,
91	        export_symbols,
92	    })
93	}
94	
95	fn elf_soname(elf: &ElfFile64<Endianness>, data: &[u8]) -> Option<String> {
96	    let endian = elf.endian();
97	    let (dynamic, dyn_index) = elf.elf_section_table().dynamic(endian, data).ok()??;
98	    let strings = elf
99	        .elf_section_table()
100	        .strings(endian, data, dyn_index)
101	        .ok()?;
102	    for d in dynamic {
103	        if d.tag32(endian) == Some(elf::DT_SONAME as u32) {
104	            if let Ok(name) = d.string(endian, strings) {
105	                return Some(String::from_utf8_lossy(name).into_owned());
106	            }
107	        }
108	    }
109	    None
110	}
111	
112	#[cfg(test)]
113	mod tests {

31	
32	pub fn parse_object(arena: &mut InputArena, path: &Path) -> Result<InputObject> {
33	    let file = std::fs::File::open(path).map_err(|e| ObjectError::Io {
34	        path: path.display().to_string(),
35	        source: e,
36	    })?;
37	    let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file) }.map_err(|e| ObjectError::Io {
38	        path: path.display().to_string(),
39	        source: e,
40	    })?;
41	    let file_id = arena.push_mmap(mmap);
42	    let bytes = arena.mmap_bytes(file_id);
43	    let bytes: &[u8] = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(bytes.as_ptr(), bytes.len()) };
44	    parse_bytes_into(arena, file_id, path.display().to_string(), bytes)
45	}
46	
47	pub fn parse_bytes_into(
48	    arena: &mut InputArena,
49	    file_id: u32,
50	    path: String,
51	    data: &[u8],
52	) -> Result<InputObject> {
53	    let base = arena.owned.len() as u32;
54	    let mut sink = Vec::new();
55	    let mut obj = parse_backed(DataSrc::Mmap(file_id), base, 0, &mut sink, path, data)?;
56	    arena.owned.extend(sink);
57	    arena.intern_object_names(&mut obj);
58	    Ok(obj)
59	}
60	
61	pub fn parse_owned_member(
62	    arena: &mut InputArena,
63	    owned_id: u32,
64	    path: String,
65	    data: &[u8],
66	) -> Result<InputObject> {
67	    let base = arena.owned.len() as u32;
68	    let mut sink = Vec::new();
69	    let mut obj = parse_backed(DataSrc::Owned(owned_id), base, 0, &mut sink, path, data)?;
70	    arena.owned.extend(sink);
71	    arena.intern_object_names(&mut obj);
72	    Ok(obj)
73	}
74	
75	pub fn parse_bare_parallel(
76	    file_id: u32,
77	    owned_base: u32,
78	    path: String,
79	    data: &[u8],
80	) -> Result<(InputObject, Vec<Vec<u8>>)> {
81	    let mut sink = Vec::new();
82	    let obj = parse_backed(DataSrc::Mmap(file_id), owned_base, 0, &mut sink, path, data)?;
83	    Ok((obj, sink))
84	}
85	
86	fn parse_backed(
87	    backing: DataSrc,
88	    owned_base: u32,
89	    base_off: u64,
90	    owned_sink: &mut Vec<Vec<u8>>,
91	    path: String,
92	    data: &[u8],
93	) -> Result<InputObject> {
94	    let elf: ElfFile64<Endianness> = ElfFile64::parse(data).map_err(|e| ObjectError::Parse {
95	        path: path.clone(),
96	        source: e,
97	    })?;
98	
99	    let section_count = elf.sections().count();
100	    let mut sections = Vec::with_capacity(section_count);
101	    let mut section_map = FxHashMap::default();
102	    section_map.reserve(section_count);
103	    let endian = elf.endian();
104	
105	    for section in elf.sections() {
106	        let idx = section.index();
107	        let sh_type = section.elf_section_header().sh_type(endian);
108	        let input_name = section.name_bytes().unwrap_or(b"");
109	        let is_debug = is_debug_section_name(input_name);
110	        let name = normalize_debug_section_name(input_name);
111	        let sh_flags = match section.flags() {
112	            object::SectionFlags::Elf { sh_flags } => sh_flags,
113	            _ => 0,
114	        };
115	        let kind = classify_section(&name, sh_flags);
116	
117	        let (file_off, file_len) = section.file_range().unwrap_or((0, 0));
118	        let is_compressed = matches!(
119	            section.compressed_file_range(),
120	            Ok(r) if r.format != object::CompressionFormat::None
121	        );
122	
123	        let mut data_handle: SectionData;
124	        let mut owned_len: Option<u64> = None;
125	        if is_debug && is_compressed {
126	            let bytes = section
127	                .uncompressed_data()
128	                .map_err(|e| ObjectError::Parse {
129	                    path: path.clone(),
130	                    source: e,
131	                })?
132	                .into_owned();
133	            let len = bytes.len();
134	            assert!(len <= u32::MAX as usize, "section too large for u32 len");
135	            let oid = owned_base + owned_sink.len() as u32;
136	            owned_sink.push(bytes);
137	            owned_len = Some(len as u64);
138	            data_handle = SectionData {
139	                src: DataSrc::Owned(oid),
140	                off: 0,
141	                len: len as u32,
142	            };
143	        } else if sh_type == elf::SHT_NOBITS || file_len == 0 {
144	            data_handle = SectionData::EMPTY;
145	        } else {
146	            let abs_off = base_off + file_off;
147	            assert!(
148	                abs_off + file_len <= u32::MAX as u64,
149	                "section offset+len {} exceeds u32 (archive/object > 4GiB not supported)",
150	                abs_off + file_len
151	            );
152	            data_handle = SectionData {
153	                src: backing,
154	                off: abs_off as u32,
155	                len: file_len as u32,
156	            };
157	        }
158	        let relocs = collect_relocs(&section);
159	
160	        let mut size = if is_debug {
161	            owned_len.unwrap_or(file_len)
162	        } else {
163	            section.size()
164	        };
165	        if kind == SectionKind::EhFrame {
166	            let raw_data = &data[file_off as usize..(file_off + file_len) as usize];
167	            let (cies, fdes, terms, leftover) = scan_eh_frame(raw_data);
168	            let ends_with_terminator = ends_with_eh_terminator(raw_data);
169	            tracing::trace!(
170	                obj = %path,
171	                input_size = raw_data.len(),
172	                sh_size = size,
173	                cies,
174	                fdes,
175	                terms,
176	                leftover,
177	                ends_with_terminator,
178	                "parse .eh_frame contribution"
179	            );
180	            if ends_with_terminator {
181	                data_handle.len = data_handle.len.saturating_sub(4);
182	                size = size.saturating_sub(4);
183	            }
184	        }
185	
186	        let isec = InputSection {
187	            index: idx,
188	            name,
189	            kind,
190	            sh_type,
191	            data: data_handle,
192	            align: section.align(),
193	            size,
194	            flags: sh_flags,
195	            relocs,
196	        };
197	        section_map.insert(idx.0, sections.len());
198	        sections.push(isec);
199	    }
200	
201	    let symbol_count = elf.symbols().count();
202	    let mut symbols = Vec::with_capacity(symbol_count);
203	    let mut symbol_map = FxHashMap::default();
204	    symbol_map.reserve(symbol_count);
205	    let elf_symtab = elf.elf_symbol_table();
206	
207	    for sym in elf.symbols() {
208	        let idx = sym.index();
209	        let name = Name::from_slice(sym.name_bytes().unwrap_or(b""));
210	        let binding = if sym.scope() == object::SymbolScope::Compilation {
211	            Binding::Local
212	        } else if sym.is_weak() {
213	            Binding::Weak
214	        } else {
215	            Binding::Global
216	        };
217	        let raw = elf_symtab.symbol(idx).ok();
218	        let st_type = raw.map(|s| s.st_type()).unwrap_or(elf::STT_NOTYPE);
219	        let visibility = raw.map(|s| s.st_visibility()).unwrap_or(elf::STV_DEFAULT);
220	        let is_ifunc = st_type == elf::STT_GNU_IFUNC;
221	        let isym = InputSymbol {
222	            index: idx,
223	            name,
224	            binding,
225	            is_undefined: sym.is_undefined(),
226	            is_common: sym.is_common(),
227	            is_ifunc,
228	            st_type,
229	            visibility,
230	            section: sym.section_index(),
231	            value: sym.address(),
232	            size: sym.size(),
233	        };
234	        symbol_map.insert(idx.0, symbols.len());
235	        symbols.push(isym);
236	    }
237	
238	    let comdat_groups = parse_comdat_groups(&elf);
239	
240	    Ok(InputObject {
241	        path,
242	        sections,
243	        symbols,
244	        section_map,
245	        symbol_map,
246	        comdat_groups,
247	    })
248	}
249

## collect_dynamic_data_relocs (function)

**Location:** peony-reloc/src/lib.rs:688
**Signature:** `(
    objects: &[InputObject],
    symbols: &SymbolTable,
    layout: &Layout,

```rust
688	pub fn collect_dynamic_data_relocs(
689	    objects: &[InputObject],
690	    symbols: &SymbolTable,
691	    layout: &Layout,
692	) -> (Vec<(u64, u64)>, Vec<(u64, u64)>) {
693	    let mut out = Vec::new();
694	    let mut ifunc = Vec::new();
695	    for (obj_id, obj) in objects.iter().enumerate() {
696	        for sec in &obj.sections {
697	            // Only allocated sections land in the loaded image.
698	            if sec.flags & peony_object::elf::SHF_ALLOC == 0 {
699	                continue;
700	            }
701	            for reloc in &sec.relocs {
702	                if reloc.r_type != r_x86_64::R64 {
703	                    continue;
704	                }
705	                let Some(sym) = obj
706	                    .symbols
707	                    .get(*obj.symbol_map.get(&reloc.symbol.0).unwrap_or(&usize::MAX))
708	                else {
709	                    continue;
710	                };
711	                // Resolve the target VA exactly as the apply phase will, and note
712	                // whether the target is an IFUNC (→ IRELATIVE, not RELATIVE).
713	                let (target, is_ifunc) = if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
714	                    match sym.section.and_then(|si| layout.address_of(obj_id, si.0)) {
715	                        Some(va) => (va + sym.value, sym.is_ifunc),
716	                        None => continue, // absolute or dropped — no base bias needed
717	                    }
718	                } else {
719	                    match symbols.lookup(&sym.name) {
720	                        // Normal imports are handled by GLOB_DAT, not RELATIVE.
721	                        // COPY imports have executable-owned storage and therefore
722	                        // behave like output-defined data for base-bias purposes.
723	                        Some(r) if r.is_defined() && (!r.import || r.copy_reloc) => {
724	                            (r.virtual_address, r.is_ifunc)
725	                        }
726	                        _ => continue,
727	                    }
728	                };
729	                let Some(site_va) = layout.address_of(obj_id, sec.index.0) else {
730	                    continue;
731	                };
732	                let site = site_va + reloc.offset;
733	                if is_ifunc {
734	                    // IRELATIVE r_addend must be the bare resolver address: the
735	                    // loader computes `*site = resolver(base + addend)`. Folding
736	                    // the input reloc addend in would call into the middle of the
737	                    // resolver. (A non-zero addend on an IFUNC R64 is not
738	                    // meaningful; ignore it.)
739	                    ifunc.push((site, target));
740	                } else {
741	                    let value = (target as i64).wrapping_add(reloc.addend) as u64;
742	                    out.push((site, value));
743	                }
744	            }
745	        }
746	    }
747	
748	    // GOT slots holding the address of a locally-defined (non-import) symbol also
749	    // need a dynamic relocation in a PIE: a plain RELATIVE for a normal symbol
750	    // (the slot holds an absolute VA the loader biases), or an IRELATIVE for an
751	    // IFUNC (the loader runs the resolver). Import GOT slots use GLOB_DAT.
752	    for (i, id) in layout.got_slots.iter().enumerate() {
753	        let Some(name) = symbols.name_by_id(*id) else {
754	            continue;
755	        };
756	        let Some(res) = symbols.lookup(name) else {
757	            continue;
758	        };
759	        if (res.import && !res.copy_reloc) || !res.is_defined() {
760	            continue;
761	        }
762	        let site = layout.got_base + (i as u64) * 8;
763	        if res.is_ifunc {
764	            ifunc.push((site, res.virtual_address));
765	        } else {
766	            out.push((site, res.virtual_address));
767	        }
768	    }
769	
770	    // Deterministic order (by site) for reproducible output + DT_RELACOUNT runs.
771	    out.sort_unstable();
772	    ifunc.sort_unstable();
773	    (out, ifunc)
774	}
```
