## write_section_data_parallel (function)

**Location:** peony-emit/src/lib.rs:444
**Signature:** `(
    buf: &mut [u8],
    arena: &InputArena,
    objects: &[InputObject],
    symbols: &SymbolTable,
    layout: &Layout,
    output_path: &Path,
) -> Result<()>`

```rust
444	fn write_section_data_parallel(
445	    buf: &mut [u8],
446	    arena: &InputArena,
447	    objects: &[InputObject],
448	    symbols: &SymbolTable,
449	    layout: &Layout,
450	    output_path: &Path,
451	) -> Result<()> {
452	    // Build a list of (file_offset, size, SecSource) tuples for each section.
453	    // We need to split the buf into disjoint mutable slices; we do this by
454	    // collecting (offset, len) pairs sorted by offset, then using split_at_mut.
455	    //
456	    // For correctness: synthetic sections (GOT, symtab, etc.) are written
457	    // serially because they reference shared `layout` data. Input sections
458	    // (the bulk of the data) are written in parallel.
459	
460	    // Phase 1: Write all synthetic sections serially (fast — small data,
461	    // except build-id which hashes all input bytes; framed separately).
462	    let synth = peony_prof::trace("emit:synthetic-sections");
463	    for sec in &layout.output_sections {
464	        match sec.source {
465	            SecSource::Input => {} // handled in phase 2
466	            SecSource::Bss => {}   // NOBITS
467	            SecSource::Got => {
468	                for (i, &sym_id) in layout.got_slots.iter().enumerate() {
469	                    let va = symbols
470	                        .name_by_id(sym_id)
471	                        .and_then(|n| symbols.lookup(n))
472	                        .map(|r| r.virtual_address)
473	                        .unwrap_or(0);
474	                    let off = (sec.sh_offset + (i as u64) * 8) as usize;
475	                    if off + 8 <= buf.len() {
476	                        buf[off..off + 8].copy_from_slice(&va.to_le_bytes());
477	                    }
478	                }
479	            }
480	            SecSource::SymTab => write_symtab(buf, symbols, layout, sec.sh_offset),
481	            SecSource::SymTabShndx => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.symtab_shndx),
482	            SecSource::StrTab => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.strtab),
483	            SecSource::ShStrTab => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.shstrtab),
484	            // Header only; the descriptor is hashed from the final image by
485	            // finalize_build_id after all bytes are written.
486	            SecSource::NoteBuildId => write_build_id(buf, sec.sh_offset),
487	            SecSource::NoteGnuProperty => {
488	                write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.gnu_property_note)
489	            }
490	            SecSource::Interp => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.interp),
491	            SecSource::Hash => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.hash),
492	            SecSource::DynSym => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.dynsym),
493	            SecSource::DynStr => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.dynstr),
494	            SecSource::RelaDyn => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.rela_dyn),
495	            SecSource::GnuVersion => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.gnu_version),
496	            SecSource::GnuVersionR => {
497	                write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.gnu_version_r)
498	            }
499	            // `.eh_frame_hdr` is filled by `write_eh_frame_hdr` after relocations.
500	            SecSource::EhFrameHdr => {}
501	            SecSource::GnuHash => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.gnu_hash),
502	            SecSource::Dynamic => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.dynamic),
503	            SecSource::Plt => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.plt),
504	            SecSource::GotPlt => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.got_plt),
505	            SecSource::RelaPlt => write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, &layout.dyn_blobs.rela_plt),
506	            SecSource::RelaEmit(idx) => {
507	                if let Some(bytes) = layout.emit_relocs.get(idx) {
508	                    write_bytes(buf, sec.sh_offset, bytes);
509	                }
510	            }
511	        }
512	    }
513	    drop(synth);
514	
515	    // Phase 2: Parallel input section copy + relocation apply.
516	    let buf_ptr = buf.as_mut_ptr() as usize;
517	    let buf_len = buf.len();
518	    // Integer-indexed symbol resolution for the per-relocation hot path: built
519	    // once here (≈49k name hashes) to replace ≈216k per-reloc name hashes with
520	    // array indexing. Borrows objects + symbols, which outlive this scope.
521	    let sym_index = {
522	        let _t = peony_prof::trace("emit:sym-index-build");
523	        peony_reloc::SymIndex::build(objects, symbols)
524	    };
525	    let ctx = ApplyCtx {
526	        symbols,
527	        layout,
528	        shared: layout.shared,
529	        sym_index: Some(&sym_index),
530	    };
531	    let work_items = collect_input_work_items(layout);
532	    peony_prof::record_items("emit", work_items.len() as u64);
533	    let _t = peony_prof::trace("emit:section-copy-dispatch");
534	    dispatch_parallel(
535	        work_items,
536	        arena,
537	        objects,
538	        buf_ptr,
539	        buf_len,
540	        ctx,
541	        output_path,
542	    )
543	}
```

## Assistant (Hephaestus - Deep Agent · GPT-5.5 · 16.7s)

_Thinking:_



Root cause is now concrete in Peony terms: executable TLS dynamic relocations were disabled (`counts.tls = 0` for non-shared), so imported DSO TLS symbols like libstdc++’s `__once_callable` were left as zero instead of getting loader-filled `TPOFF64` entries. I’m going to patch Peony to emit executable TLS relocs for imported TLS refs.

## count_tls_relocs (function)

**Location:** peony-reloc/src/lib.rs:915
**Signature:** `(
    objects: &[InputObject],
    symbols: &SymbolTable,
    tls: &peony_layout::TlsGotInfo,
) -> usize`

```rust
915	pub fn count_tls_relocs(
916	    objects: &[InputObject],
917	    symbols: &SymbolTable,
918	    tls: &peony_layout::TlsGotInfo,
919	) -> usize {
920	    let imported = |tref: &TlsRef| -> bool {
921	        match tref {
922	            TlsRef::Local(..) => false,
923	            TlsRef::Global(id) => symbols
924	                .name_by_id(*id)
925	                .and_then(|n| symbols.lookup(n))
926	                .map(|r| r.defined_in.is_none())
927	                .unwrap_or(true),
928	        }
929	    };
930	    let _ = objects;
931	    let gd: usize = tls.gd.iter().map(|t| if imported(t) { 2 } else { 1 }).sum();
932	    let desc = tls.desc.len();
933	    let ie = tls.ie.len();
934	    let ldm = usize::from(tls.ldm);
935	    gd + desc + ie + ldm
936	}
```

## apply_reloc (function)

**Location:** peony-reloc/src/lib.rs:1094
**Signature:** `(
    ctx: &ApplyCtx<'_>,
    obj: &InputObject,
    obj_id: usize,
    reloc: &InputReloc,
    section_va: u64,
    buf: &mut [u8],
) -> Result<()>`

```rust
1094	pub fn apply_reloc(
1095	    ctx: &ApplyCtx<'_>,
1096	    obj: &InputObject,
1097	    obj_id: usize,
1098	    reloc: &InputReloc,
1099	    section_va: u64,
1100	    buf: &mut [u8],
1101	) -> Result<()> {
1102	    if reloc.r_type == r_x86_64::NONE {
1103	        return Ok(());
1104	    }
1105	    let sym_pos = *obj.symbol_map.get(&reloc.symbol.0).unwrap_or(&usize::MAX);
1106	    let Some(sym) = obj.symbols.get(sym_pos) else {
1107	        return Ok(());
1108	    };
1109	
1110	    // In an EXECUTABLE the `call __tls_get_addr@PLT` after a GD/LD `lea` is
1111	    // rewritten by the TLSGD/TLSLD→LE relaxation, so its PLT32/PC32 relocation
1112	    // must not run (it would corrupt the relaxed Local-Exec bytes). In a SHARED
1113	    // object the call is KEPT, so the relocation must run normally (resolve the
1114	    // real PLT stub) — do not skip it there.
1115	    if !ctx.shared
1116	        && matches!(reloc.r_type, r_x86_64::PLT32 | r_x86_64::PC32)
1117	        && sym.name == b"__tls_get_addr"
1118	    {
1119	        return Ok(());
1120	    }
1121	
1122	    // Resolve the global symbol's record ONCE (a name-keyed hash lookup) and
1123	    // reuse it for the address/GOT/PLT, the TLS offset, and the TLS-GOT keying
1124	    // below. apply_reloc runs per relocation, and this `sym` is the same across
1125	    // all three uses, so hashing the mangled name once instead of up to 3× is a
1126	    // free, local win (no behaviour change — guarded by the byte-identical
1127	    // determinism tests). Locals never enter the global table.
1128	    let res = if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
1129	        None
1130	    } else if let Some(index) = ctx.sym_index {
1131	        // Integer-indexed resolution (no name hash). Shadow-checked against the
1132	        // name path in debug builds to prove the index is faithful.
1133	        let by_id = index.resolution(obj_id, sym_pos);
1134	        debug_assert!(
1135	            by_id.map(|r| r.id) == ctx.symbols.lookup(&sym.name).map(|r| r.id),
1136	            "sym_index disagrees with name lookup for `{}`",
1137	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&sym.name)
1138	        );
1139	        by_id
1140	    } else {
1141	        ctx.symbols.lookup(&sym.name)
1142	    };
1143	
1144	    // Resolve the symbol's address (S), GOT slot (G), PLT (L), size (Z).
1145	    let (s, g, l, z) = if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
1146	        let s = match sym.section {
1147	            Some(si) => match ctx.layout.address_of(obj_id, si.0) {
1148	                Some(va) => va + sym.value,
1149	                None => return Ok(()), // defined in a dropped section
1150	            },
1151	            None => sym.value, // absolute local
1152	        };
1153	        (s, 0, 0, sym.size)
1154	    } else {
1155	        match res {
1156	            Some(r) if r.is_defined() => (r.virtual_address, r.got_address, r.plt_address, r.size),
1157	            // Weak-undefined: address resolves to 0, but a GOT reference still
1158	            // uses the symbol's allocated GOT slot (which holds 0). Passing the
1159	            // real `got_address` lets `mov sym@GOTPCREL,%rax; test %rax,%rax`
1160	            // correctly observe null instead of dereferencing a bogus slot.
1161	            Some(r) if sym.binding == Binding::Weak => (0, r.got_address, 0, 0),
1162	            Some(_) if sym.binding == Binding::Weak => (0, 0, 0, 0),
1163	            _ => {
1164	                return Err(RelocError::UndefinedSymbol {
1165	                    name: String::from_utf8_lossy(&sym.name).into_owned(),
1166	                    object: obj.path.clone(),
1167	                });
1168	            }
1169	        }
1170	    };
1171	
1172	    // For TLS relocations, compute the symbol's offset within the TLS block.
1173	    let tls = if is_tls(reloc.r_type) {
1174	        if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
1175	            sym.section
1176	                .and_then(|si| ctx.layout.tls_offset(obj_id, si.0))
1177	                .map(|b| b + sym.value)
1178	        } else {
1179	            res.and_then(|r| {
1180	                let def = r.defined_in?;
1181	                r.section_index
1182	                    .and_then(|si| ctx.layout.tls_offset(def.0 as usize, si))
1183	                    .map(|b| b + r.value)
1184	            })
1185	        }
1186	        .unwrap_or(0)
1187	    } else {
1188	        0
1189	    };
1190	
1191	    // TLS GOT addresses for this reference, keyed by `TlsRef` exactly as the
1192	    // scan allocated them. IE (GOTTPOFF) slots exist in BOTH exe and shared
1193	    // outputs; GD/LDM pairs only in a shared object.
1194	    let (tls_gd, tls_ie, tls_desc) = {
1195	        let sym_pos = obj.symbol_map.get(&reloc.symbol.0).copied();
1196	        let tref = match sym_pos {
1197	            Some(pos) if sym.binding == Binding::Local => Some(TlsRef::Local(obj_id, pos)),
1198	            Some(pos) => match res {
1199	                Some(r) => Some(TlsRef::Global(r.id)),
1200	                None => Some(TlsRef::Local(obj_id, pos)),
1201	            },
1202	            None => None,
1203	        };
1204	        let gd = tref
1205	            .and_then(|t| ctx.layout.tls_gd_addr.get(&t).copied())
1206	            .unwrap_or(0);
1207	        let ie = tref
1208	            .and_then(|t| ctx.layout.tls_ie_addr.get(&t).copied())
1209	            .unwrap_or(0);
1210	        let desc = tref
1211	            .and_then(|t| ctx.layout.tls_desc_addr.get(&t).copied())
1212	            .unwrap_or(0);
1213	        (gd, ie, desc)
1214	    };
1215	
1216	    let addrs = RelocAddrs {
1217	        s,
1218	        a: reloc.addend,
1219	        p: section_va + reloc.offset,
1220	        g,
1221	        l,
1222	        z,
1223	        got_base: ctx.layout.got_base,
1224	        tls,
1225	        tls_size: ctx.layout.tls_size,
1226	        offset: reloc.offset as usize,
1227	        shared: ctx.shared,
1228	        tls_gd,
1229	        tls_ie,
1230	        tls_desc,
1231	        tls_ldm: ctx.layout.tls_ldm_addr.unwrap_or(0),
1232	    };
1233	
1234	    patch_buf(buf, reloc.r_type, &addrs, &obj.path)
1235	}
```

48	// ── x86-64 relocation type constants ─────────────────────────────────────────
49	
50	pub mod r_x86_64 {
51	    pub const NONE: u32 = 0;
52	    pub const R64: u32 = 1;
53	    pub const PC32: u32 = 2;
54	    pub const GOT32: u32 = 3;
55	    pub const PLT32: u32 = 4;
56	    pub const GOTPCREL: u32 = 9;
57	    pub const R32: u32 = 10;
58	    pub const R32S: u32 = 11;
59	    pub const R16: u32 = 12;
60	    pub const PC16: u32 = 13;
61	    pub const R8: u32 = 14;
62	    pub const PC8: u32 = 15;
63	    pub const DTPMOD64: u32 = 16; // TLS module id (GD/LDM GOT slot0), loader-filled
64	    pub const DTPOFF64: u32 = 17;
65	    pub const TPOFF64: u32 = 18;
66	    pub const TLSGD: u32 = 19; // General-Dynamic: relaxed to Local-Exec in an exe
67	    pub const TLSLD: u32 = 20; // Local-Dynamic: relaxed to Local-Exec in an exe

... (gap) ...

540	/// IFUNC (IRELATIVE) R64 sites — i.e. it matches the union of the RELATIVE and
541	/// IRELATIVE lists returned by [`collect_dynamic_data_relocs`]; use
542	/// [`count_irelative`] to recover the IFUNC subset.
543	pub fn count_relative(objects: &[InputObject], symbols: &SymbolTable) -> usize {
544	    let mut n = 0;
545	    for obj in objects {
546	        for sec in &obj.sections {
547	            if sec.flags & peony_object::elf::SHF_ALLOC == 0 {
548	                continue;
549	            }
550	            for reloc in &sec.relocs {
551	                if reloc.r_type != r_x86_64::R64 {
552	                    continue;
553	                }
554	                let Some(sym) = obj
555	                    .symbols
556	                    .get(*obj.symbol_map.get(&reloc.symbol.0).unwrap_or(&usize::MAX))
557	                else {
558	                    continue;
559	                };
560	                let counts = if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
561	                    // Local defined in a kept, allocated section.
562	                    sym.section.is_some()
563	                } else {
564	                    symbols
565	                        .lookup(&sym.name)
566	                        .is_some_and(|r| r.is_defined() && (!r.import || r.copy_reloc))
567	                };
568	                if counts {
569	                    n += 1;
570	                }
571	            }
572	        }
573	    }
574	    n
575	}

## write_tls_got (function)

**Location:** peony-emit/src/lib.rs:210
**Signature:** `(buf: &mut [u8], layout: &Layout)`

```rust
210	fn write_tls_got(buf: &mut [u8], layout: &Layout) {
211	    if layout.tls_got_writes.is_empty() {
212	        return;
213	    }
214	    let Some(got) = layout
215	        .output_sections
216	        .iter()
217	        .find(|s| s.source == SecSource::Got)
218	    else {
219	        return;
220	    };
221	    let (lo, hi) = (got.sh_addr, got.sh_addr + got.sh_size);
222	    for &(va, value) in &layout.tls_got_writes {
223	        if va < lo || va + 8 > hi {
224	            tracing::warn!(
225	                va = format_args!("{va:#x}"),
226	                "TLS GOT static write outside .got — skipping"
227	            );
228	            continue;
229	        }
230	        let file_off = (got.sh_offset + (va - got.sh_addr)) as usize;
231	        if file_off + 8 <= buf.len() {
232	            buf[file_off..file_off + 8].copy_from_slice(&value.to_le_bytes());
233	            tracing::trace!(
234	                va = format_args!("{va:#x}"),
235	                value,
236	                "TLS GOT static write (DTPOFF)"
237	            );
238	        }
239	    }
240	}
```

## collect_symbolic_data_relocs (function)

**Location:** peony-reloc/src/lib.rs:623
**Signature:** `(
    objects: &[InputObject],
    symbols: &SymbolTable,
    layout: &Layout,

```rust
623	pub fn collect_symbolic_data_relocs(
624	    objects: &[InputObject],
625	    symbols: &SymbolTable,
626	    layout: &Layout,
627	) -> Vec<(u64, u32, i64)> {
628	    let mut out = Vec::new();
629	    for (obj_id, obj) in objects.iter().enumerate() {
630	        for sec in &obj.sections {
631	            if sec.flags & peony_object::elf::SHF_ALLOC == 0 {
632	                continue;
633	            }
634	            for reloc in &sec.relocs {
635	                if reloc.r_type != r_x86_64::R64 {
636	                    continue;
637	                }
638	                let Some(sym) = obj
639	                    .symbols
640	                    .get(*obj.symbol_map.get(&reloc.symbol.0).unwrap_or(&usize::MAX))
641	                else {
642	                    continue;
643	                };
644	                if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
645	                    continue;
646	                }
647	                let Some(res) = symbols.lookup(&sym.name) else {
648	                    continue;
649	                };
650	                if !res.import || res.copy_reloc || res.dynsym_index == 0 {
651	                    continue;
652	                }
653	                let Some(site_va) = layout.address_of(obj_id, sec.index.0) else {
654	                    continue;
655	                };
656	                out.push((site_va + reloc.offset, res.dynsym_index, reloc.addend));
657	            }
658	        }
659	    }
660	    out.sort_unstable();
661	    out
662	}
```

1251

1333	        // DTPOFF: offset of the symbol within its module's TLS block.
1334	        //
1335	        // In a SHARED object the matching `lea x@tlsld(%rip)` + `call
1336	        // __tls_get_addr` is KEPT, and the helper returns the module's TLS block
1337	        // base, so DTPOFF stays module-relative (`tls + addend`).

1339	        // In an EXECUTABLE peony relaxes the Local-Dynamic sequence to

1370	        }
1371	
1372	        // ── TLS access-model relaxation (executable ⇒ Local-Exec) ────────────
1373	        //
1374	        // In an executable the static TLS block is fixed, so General-Dynamic and
1375	        // Local-Dynamic accesses are relaxed to Local-Exec, eliminating the
1376	        // runtime `__tls_get_addr` call. We rewrite the fixed instruction
1377	        // sequence the compiler emits and patch the TP-relative offset.

1402	                // R_X86_64_TLSGD is encoded as a PC-relative LEA displacement,

1407	                    .wrapping_add(a.a)

1416	                    le_off,

1418	                );

1444	        // the module LDM GOT pair; the per-symbol offset is added by the

1453	            );

1455	        }

1457	            // LD→LE relaxation. The input sequence is:

1502	            tracing::trace!(

1515	                disp = v,

1517	            );

1519	        }
1520	        GOTPC32_TLSDESC => {

## Assistant (Hephaestus - Deep Agent · GPT-5.5 · 14.5s)

_Thinking:_



Yes: with `/usr/bin/cc` it passes, and with Peony it crashes. The linker bug is Peony dropping executable dynamic TLS relocations for imported TLS symbols, so libstdc++’s `std::call_once` TLS slots stay zero and ONNX Runtime jumps through a null callback. I’m patching that in Peony now.

<path>/home/cole/RustProjects/active/peony/peony-reloc/src/lib.rs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
822: /// Build the TLS GOT region's dynamic relocations and static slot writes from
823: /// the addresses the layout assigned. Must run post-layout. `shared` selects
824: /// dynamic (GD/LD/IE with loader-filled module ids / TP offsets) vs the
825: /// executable case (Initial-Exec slots filled statically; GD/LD are relaxed
826: /// away in the executable and produce no GOT pairs here).
827: pub fn collect_tls_got(
828:     objects: &[InputObject],
829:     symbols: &SymbolTable,
830:     layout: &Layout,
831:     tls: &peony_layout::TlsGotInfo,
832:     shared: bool,
833: ) -> TlsGotContents {
834:     let mut c = TlsGotContents::default();
835:     if shared {
836:         // General-Dynamic pairs: slot0 = DTPMOD64, slot1 = DTPOFF.
837:         for tref in &tls.gd {
838:             let Some(&pair) = layout.tls_gd_addr.get(tref) else {
839:                 continue;
840:             };
841:             let (offset, imported) =
842:                 tls_ref_offset(*tref, objects, symbols, layout).unwrap_or((0, true));
843:             if imported {
844:                 // Imported TLS: both module id and offset are resolved at load.
845:                 let dynidx = tls_ref_dynidx(*tref, symbols);
846:                 c.relocs.push((pair, r_x86_64::DTPMOD64, dynidx, 0));
847:                 c.relocs.push((pair + 8, r_x86_64::DTPOFF64, dynidx, 0));
848:             } else {
849:                 // Locally-defined: DTPMOD64 (module, loader-filled) + static DTPOFF.
850:                 c.relocs.push((pair, r_x86_64::DTPMOD64, 0, 0));
851:                 c.static_writes.push((pair + 8, offset));
852:             }
853:         }
854:         // TLSDESC pairs: one dynamic relocation fills the two-word descriptor.
855:         for tref in &tls.desc {
856:             let Some(&pair) = layout.tls_desc_addr.get(tref) else {
857:                 continue;
858:             };
859:             let (offset, imported) =
860:                 tls_ref_offset(*tref, objects, symbols, layout).unwrap_or((0, true));
861:             let dynidx = if imported {
862:                 tls_ref_dynidx(*tref, symbols)
863:             } else {
864:                 0
865:             };
866:             let addend = if imported { 0 } else { offset as i64 };
867:             c.relocs.push((pair, r_x86_64::TLSDESC, dynidx, addend));
868:         }
869:         // Module Local-Dynamic pair: slot0 = DTPMOD64, slot1 = 0 (static).
870:         if let Some(ldm) = layout.tls_ldm_addr {
871:             c.relocs.push((ldm, r_x86_64::DTPMOD64, 0, 0));
872:             c.static_writes.push((ldm + 8, 0));
873:         }
874:     }
875:     // Initial-Exec slots hold the TP-relative offset of the symbol.
876:     for tref in &tls.ie {
877:         let Some(&slot) = layout.tls_ie_addr.get(tref) else {
878:             continue;
879:         };
880:         let (offset, imported) =
881:             tls_ref_offset(*tref, objects, symbols, layout).unwrap_or((0, true));
882:         if shared && imported {
883:             // Imported IE TLS: loader fills the slot (TPOFF64 against the symbol).
884:             let dynidx = tls_ref_dynidx(*tref, symbols);
885:             c.relocs.push((slot, r_x86_64::TPOFF64, dynidx, 0));
886:         } else if shared {
887:             // Locally-defined IE in a .so: loader fills the slot (module-relative
888:             // addend) since the TP offset isn't known until load.
889:             c.relocs.push((slot, r_x86_64::TPOFF64, 0, offset as i64));
890:         } else {
891:             // EXECUTABLE: the TP offset is fixed (TP is the end of the static TLS
892:             // block), so write it statically — `offset - tls_size`, negative.
893:             let tp = (offset as i64).wrapping_sub(layout.tls_size as i64);
894:             c.static_writes.push((slot, tp as u64));
895:         }
896:     }
897:     c
898: }
899: 
900: /// `.dynsym` index for an imported TLS symbol (0 if not an import / unknown).
901: fn tls_ref_dynidx(tref: TlsRef, symbols: &SymbolTable) -> u32 {
902:     if let TlsRef::Global(id) = tref {
903:         if let Some(name) = symbols.name_by_id(id) {
904:             if let Some(r) = symbols.lookup(name) {
905:                 return r.dynsym_index;
906:             }
907:         }
908:     }
909:     0
910: }
911: 
912: /// Count the TLS dynamic relocations a shared object's TLS GOT will need, to
913: /// size `.rela.dyn` before layout. Each GD pair contributes 1 (local) or 2
914: /// (imported), each TLSDESC pair 1, the LDM pair 1, each IE slot 1.
915: pub fn count_tls_relocs(
916:     objects: &[InputObject],
917:     symbols: &SymbolTable,
918:     tls: &peony_layout::TlsGotInfo,
919: ) -> usize {
920:     let imported = |tref: &TlsRef| -> bool {
921:         match tref {
922:             TlsRef::Local(..) => false,
923:             TlsRef::Global(id) => symbols
924:                 .name_by_id(*id)
925:                 .and_then(|n| symbols.lookup(n))
926:                 .map(|r| r.defined_in.is_none())
927:                 .unwrap_or(true),
928:         }
929:     };
930:     let _ = objects;
931:     let gd: usize = tls.gd.iter().map(|t| if imported(t) { 2 } else { 1 }).sum();
932:     let desc = tls.desc.len();
933:     let ie = tls.ie.len();
934:     let ldm = usize::from(tls.ldm);
935:     gd + desc + ie + ldm
936: }

</content>

398:     /// Fill the reserved leading `R_X86_64_RELATIVE` prefix of `.rela.dyn`.
399:     ///
400:     /// `relative` is `(site_vaddr, link_time_target_vaddr)` from
401:     /// [`peony_reloc::collect_relative`]. Each becomes a RELA entry with
402:     /// `r_info = R_X86_64_RELATIVE` (symbol 0) and `r_addend = target`, so the
403:     /// loader writes `*site = load_base + target`. These occupy the zero-filled
404:     /// prefix `compute_layout` reserved (RELATIVE entries precede GLOB_DAT so
405:     /// glibc's DT_RELACOUNT fast path stays valid). `relative.len()` must not
406:     /// exceed the reserved `n_relative`; any unused prefix stays `R_X86_64_NONE`.
407:     pub fn append_relative_relocs(&mut self, relative: &[(u64, u64)]) {
408:         self.append_dynamic_relocs(relative, &[]);
409:     }
410: 
411:     pub fn append_dynamic_relocs(&mut self, relative: &[(u64, u64)], irelative: &[(u64, u64)]) {
412:         self.append_all_dynamic_relocs(relative, irelative, &[], &[]);
413:     }
414: 
415:     /// Assemble `.rela.dyn`: `R_X86_64_RELATIVE` entries first (covered by
416:     /// `DT_RELACOUNT`), then `R_X86_64_IRELATIVE` IFUNC entries, then TLS
417:     /// (`DTPMOD64`/`DTPOFF64`/`TPOFF64`) entries, then the stashed
418:     /// `R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT` and `R_X86_64_COPY` imports — with no type-0 gap
419:     /// anywhere (BFD/eu-elflint reject `NONE` records). `irelative` is
420:     /// `(got_slot_va, resolver_va)`; `tls` is `(site_va, r_type, dynsym_index,
421:     /// addend)`.
422:     pub fn append_all_dynamic_relocs(
423:         &mut self,
424:         relative: &[(u64, u64)],
425:         irelative: &[(u64, u64)],
426:         tls: &[(u64, u32, u32, i64)],
427:         symbolic: &[(u64, u32, i64)],
428:     ) {
429:         let cap = self.rela_dyn_section_size() as usize;
430:         let glob_len = self.dyn_blobs.rela_glob_dat.len();
431:         let copy_len = self.dyn_blobs.rela_copy.len();
432:         let suffix_len = glob_len + copy_len;
433:         let mut bytes = Vec::with_capacity(cap);
434:         let mut written = 0u64;
435:         for &(site, target) in relative {
436:             if bytes.len() + elf::RELA_SIZE as usize > cap - suffix_len {
437:                 break; // never crowd out the IRELATIVE/TLS/GLOB_DAT/COPY suffixes
438:             }
439:             let r_info = elf::R_X86_64_RELATIVE as u64; // symbol index 0
440:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&site.to_le_bytes());
441:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&r_info.to_le_bytes());
442:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&(target as i64).to_le_bytes());
443:             written += 1;
444:         }
445:         // IRELATIVE: IFUNC GOT slots resolved by running the resolver at startup.
446:         let mut written_irel = 0usize;
447:         for &(site, resolver) in irelative {
448:             if bytes.len() + elf::RELA_SIZE as usize > cap - suffix_len {
449:                 break;
450:             }
451:             let r_info = elf::R_X86_64_IRELATIVE as u64; // symbol index 0
452:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&site.to_le_bytes());
453:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&r_info.to_le_bytes());
454:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&(resolver as i64).to_le_bytes());
455:             written_irel += 1;
456:         }
457:         if written_irel != irelative.len() {
458:             tracing::error!(
459:                 wrote = written_irel,
460:                 wanted = irelative.len(),
461:                 "BUG: .rela.dyn too small for all IRELATIVE entries — IFUNC resolution broken"
462:             );
463:             debug_assert_eq!(written_irel, irelative.len(), "IRELATIVE entries dropped");
464:         }
465:         // TLS: DTPMOD64 / DTPOFF64 / TPOFF64 GOT-pair relocations (shared object).
466:         let mut written_tls = 0usize;
467:         for &(site, r_type, dynidx, addend) in tls {
468:             if bytes.len() + elf::RELA_SIZE as usize > cap - suffix_len {
469:                 break;
470:             }
471:             let r_info = ((dynidx as u64) << 32) | (r_type as u64);
472:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&site.to_le_bytes());
473:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&r_info.to_le_bytes());
474:             bytes.extend_from_slice(&addend.to_le_bytes());
475:             written_tls += 1;
476:         }
477:         if written_tls != tls.len() {
478:             tracing::error!(
479:                 wrote = written_tls,
480:                 wanted = tls.len(),
481:                 "BUG: .rela.dyn too small for all TLS entries — thread-locals broken"
482:             );
483:             debug_assert_eq!(written_tls, tls.len(), "TLS dyn relocs dropped");
484:         }
485:         // Symbolic R_X86_64_64: data sites referencing an imported symbol (e.g.
486:         // gcc's `.data.rel.local.DW.ref.*` EH personality/typeinfo slots). The
487:         // loader writes `*site = sym_address + addend`.
488:         let mut written_sym = 0usize;
489:         for &(site, dynidx, addend) in symbolic {
490:             if bytes.len() + elf::RELA_SIZE as usize > cap - suffix_len {
491:                 break;
492:             }

493:     // the layout will apply.

536:     for (i, name) in imports.iter().enumerate() {
537:         if let Some(r) = symbols.lookup_mut(name) {

539:         }
540:     }
541:     // A PIE needs R_X86_64_RELATIVE dynamic relocations for absolute pointers,
542:     // even with no imports. Emit dynamic sections whenever there are imports OR
543:     // the output is a PIE (rustc/cc default).
544:     // Both PIE and shared objects are ET_DYN and need R_X86_64_RELATIVE dynamic
545:     // relocations for absolute pointers the loader must bias.
546:     let et_dyn = args.pie || args.shared;
547:     let dynamic_counts = if et_dyn || args.shared {
548:         let _t = peony_prof::trace("reloc-postproc:dynamic-counts");
549:         peony_reloc::count_dynamic_relocs(&objects, &symbols, &got_syms, &tls_got, args.shared)
550:     } else {
551:         peony_reloc::DynamicRelocCounts::default()
552:     };
553:     let n_relative = dynamic_counts.relative_total;
554:     let n_irelative = dynamic_counts.irelative;
555:     // A shared object exports its defined, non-hidden global/weak symbols so
556:     // `dlsym` can find them. When rustc supplies a `--version-script`, it lists
557:     // exactly the symbols to export (`global:`) and localizes the rest
558:     // (`local: *`), so we honour it as an allowlist — otherwise std's thousands
559:     // of internal globals would all leak into `.dynsym`.
560:     let version_script = match &args.version_script {
561:         Some(p) => Some(parse_version_script(p)?),
562:         None => None,
563:     };
564:     let exports: Vec<peony_layout::ExportSym> = if args.shared {
565:         let _t = peony_prof::trace("reloc-postproc:exports");
566:         let mut e: Vec<peony_layout::ExportSym> = symbols
567:             .iter()
568:             .filter(|(_, r)| r.is_export())
569:             .filter(|(name, _)| version_script.as_ref().is_none_or(|vs| vs.exports(name)))
570:             .map(|(name, r)| {
571:                 let bind = match r.binding {
572:                     peony_object::Binding::Weak => peony_object::elf::STB_WEAK,
573:                     _ => peony_object::elf::STB_GLOBAL,
574:                 };
575:                 peony_layout::ExportSym {
576:                     name: name.to_vec(),
577:                     info: peony_object::elf::st_info(bind, r.st_type),
578:                     other: r.visibility,
579:                 }
580:             })
581:             .collect();
582:         e.sort_by(|a, b| a.name.cmp(&b.name));
583:         peony_prof::count("dynamic_exports", e.len() as u64);
584:         e
585:     } else {
586:         Vec::new()
587:     };
588:     // A shared object's SONAME: explicit -soname, else the output file name.
589:     let soname = if args.shared {
590:         Some(args.soname.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {
591:             args.output
592:                 .file_name()
593:                 .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
594:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| "a.out".to_string())
595:         }))
596:     } else {
597:         None
598:     };
599:     // TLS dynamic relocations the TLS GOT will need (sizes `.rela.dyn`). A shared
600:     // object needs GD/LD/IE relocs; an executable relaxes GD/LD to Local-Exec and
601:     // fills IE slots statically, so it needs none.
602:     let n_tls_reloc = dynamic_counts.tls;
603:     // Symbolic R_X86_64_64 dynamic relocs for data sites referencing an imported
604:     // symbol (gcc's `.data.rel.local.DW.ref.*` EH slots). Sized here, collected
605:     // post-layout. Meaningful for any ET_DYN (PIE or shared).
606:     let n_symbolic_data = dynamic_counts.symbolic_data;
607:     // Dynamic sections are needed for any import, any PIE, or a shared object.
608:     let dynamic = (!imports.is_empty() || et_dyn).then(|| peony_layout::DynamicInfo {
609:         imports,
610:         import_versions,
611:         import_sonames,
612:         needed: needed.clone(),
613:         pie: args.pie,
614:         n_relative,
615:         n_irelative,
616:         shared: args.shared,
617:         soname,
618:         exports,
619:         n_tls_reloc,
620:         copy_relocs,
621:         n_symbolic_data,
622:     });
623:     if dynamic.is_some() {
624:         tracing::info!(
625:             needed = needed.len(),
626:             n_relative,
627:             shared = args.shared,
628:             "dynamic object"
629:         );
630:     }
631:     peony_prof::record_rss("after-reloc-postproc");
632: 
633:     // Predefine linker-provided symbols and `--defsym`s BEFORE layout so they are
634:     // included in `.symtab`; the layout-dependent addresses are filled in after.
635:     let provided = predefine_linker_symbols(&mut symbols);
636:     apply_defsym(&mut symbols, &args.defsym)?;
637: 
638:     let config = LayoutConfig {
639:         base_address,
640:         entry_symbol: args.entry.clone(),
641:         build_id: args.build_id,
642:         strip: args.strip_all,
643:         strip_debug: args.strip_debug || args.strip_all,
644:         pie: args.pie,
645:         shared: args.shared,
646:         emit_relocs: args.emit_relocs && !args.strip_all,
647:         script: (!script_controls.layout.output_sections.is_empty())
648:             .then_some(script_controls.layout.clone()),
649:         ..Default::default()
650:     };
651:     tracing::info!("computing layout");
652:     // --icf: fold byte+reloc-identical, non-address-significant .text sections.
653:     let fold_map = if args.icf {
654:         let fm = peony_layout::icf::compute_fold_map(&arena, &objects);
655:         if !fm.is_empty() {
656:             tracing::info!(folded = fm.len(), "ICF: folded identical sections");
657:         }
658:         Some(fm)
659:     } else {
660:         None
661:     };
662:     drop(_postscan);
663:     let layout_span = peony_prof::phase("layout");
664:     let mut layout = peony_layout::compute_layout_icf(
665:         &arena,
666:         &objects,
667:         &symbols,
668:         &got_syms,
669:         &plt_syms,
670:         live.as_filter(),
671:         dynamic.as_ref(),
672:         &config,
673:         &tls_got,
674:         fold_map.as_ref(),
675:     )
676:     .context("layout computation failed")?;
677:     drop(layout_span);
678:     peony_prof::count("layout_sections", layout.output_sections.len() as u64);
679:     peony_prof::count("layout_segments", layout.segments.len() as u64);
680:     peony_prof::record_items("layout", layout.output_sections.len() as u64);
681:     peony_prof::record_bytes("layout", layout.file_size);
682:     peony_prof::record_rss("after-layout");
683:     tracing::info!(
684:         sections = layout.output_sections.len(),
685:         segments = layout.segments.len(),
686:         file_size = layout.file_size,
687:         entry = format_args!("{:#x}", layout.entry),
688:         "layout complete"
689:     );
690: 
691:     // Post-layout symbol finalization + dynamic-reloc assembly was previously
692:     // untimed ("other"): patch linker-symbol addresses, finalize the symtab,
693:     // and (for ET_DYN) collect RELATIVE/IRELATIVE/TLS/symbolic dynamic relocs.
694:     let _finalize = peony_prof::phase("finalize-syms");
695:     set_linker_addresses(&mut symbols, &layout, &provided);
696:     finalize_symbols(&mut symbols, &layout);
697:     peony_prof::record_items("finalize-syms", symbols.len() as u64);
698:     // A shared object may legitimately reference symbols it does not define;
699:     // they are resolved at load time against the process image. Only enforce
700:     // full resolution for executables.
701:     if !args.shared {
702:         check_undefined(&symbols).context("unresolved symbols")?;
703:     }
704: 
705:     // Assemble `.rela.dyn` now that symbol VAs are final: the R_X86_64_RELATIVE
706:     // entries (ET_DYN only) come first, then the GLOB_DATs. For a non-PIE dynamic
707:     // executable there are no relatives, so this just materialises the GLOB_DATs.
708:     if dynamic.is_some() {
709:         // Partition data relocations into RELATIVE (normal) and IRELATIVE (IFUNC,
710:         // resolver run at startup). Meaningful for any ET_DYN (PIE or shared); a
711:         // non-PIE dynamic exe has no base-relative data relocs.
712:         let (relative, irelative) = if et_dyn {
713:             peony_reloc::collect_dynamic_data_relocs(&objects, &symbols, &layout)
714:         } else {
715:             (Vec::new(), Vec::new())
716:         };
717:         if !irelative.is_empty() {
718:             tracing::info!(
719:                 ifuncs = irelative.len(),
720:                 "emitting R_X86_64_IRELATIVE relocations"
721:             );
722:         }
723:         // TLS GOT contents: a shared object emits DTPMOD64/DTPOFF64/TPOFF64
724:         // dynamic relocs (+ static DTPOFF in GD/LDM slot1); an executable writes
725:         // its Initial-Exec slots statically (no relocs). Always run when there
726:         // are TLS GOT slots so exe IE slots get filled.
727:         let tls_dyn: Vec<(u64, u32, u32, i64)>;
728:         if !tls_got.is_empty() {
729:             let contents =
730:                 peony_reloc::collect_tls_got(&objects, &symbols, &layout, &tls_got, args.shared);
731:             tracing::info!(
732:                 tls_relocs = contents.relocs.len(),
733:                 tls_static = contents.static_writes.len(),
734:                 shared = args.shared,
735:                 "emitting TLS GOT (GD/LD/IE)"
736:             );
737:             layout.tls_got_writes = contents.static_writes;
738:             tls_dyn = contents.relocs;
739:         } else {
740:             tls_dyn = Vec::new();
741:         }
742:         // Symbolic R_X86_64_64 dynamic relocs for imported-symbol data sites.
743:         let symbolic = if et_dyn {
744:             peony_reloc::collect_symbolic_data_relocs(&objects, &symbols, &layout)

(Showing lines 430-744 of 2024. Use offset=745 to continue.)

1: //! Thread-Local Storage (Local-Exec model) tests. A freestanding program sets up
2: //! `%fs` itself (`arch_prctl`) and accesses a `.tbss` variable via `@tpoff`
3: //! (`R_X86_64_TPOFF32`); peony must emit `PT_TLS` and compute the TP offset.
4: 
5: mod common;
6: use common::*;
7: 
8: #[test]
9: fn tls_local_exec() {
10:     let dir = workdir("tls");
11:     let o = assemble(
12:         &dir,
13:         "t",
14:         "
15:         .section .tbss,\"awT\",@nobits
16:         .globl tv
17:         .align 4
18:     tv:
19:         .zero 4
20:         .text
21:         .globl _start
22:     _start:
23:         leaq buf(%rip), %rsi
24:         addq $4096, %rsi          # TP = buf+4096; TP + tpoff lands inside buf
25:         movl $158, %eax           # arch_prctl
26:         movl $0x1002, %edi        # ARCH_SET_FS
27:         syscall
28:         movl $42, %fs:tv@tpoff    # R_X86_64_TPOFF32
29:         movl %fs:tv@tpoff, %edi
30:         movl $60, %eax
31:         syscall
32:         .bss
33:         .align 64
34:     buf:
35:         .zero 8192
36:     ",
37:     );
38:     let exe = dir.join("a.out");
39:     link(&exe, &[o], &[]);
40:     assert!(readelf(&exe, &["-l"]).contains("TLS"), "missing PT_TLS");
41:     assert_eq!(run(&exe), 42);
42: }
43: 
44: /// Two TLS variables in `.tbss` get distinct TP offsets.
45: #[test]
46: fn tls_two_vars() {
47:     let dir = workdir("tls2");
48:     let o = assemble(
49:         &dir,
50:         "t",
51:         "
52:         .section .tbss,\"awT\",@nobits
53:         .globl a
54:         .globl b
55:         .align 4
56:     a:
57:         .zero 4
58:     b:
59:         .zero 4
60:         .text
61:         .globl _start
62:     _start:
63:         leaq buf(%rip), %rsi
64:         addq $4096, %rsi
65:         movl $158, %eax
66:         movl $0x1002, %edi
67:         syscall
68:         movl $40, %fs:a@tpoff
69:         movl $2,  %fs:b@tpoff
70:         movl %fs:a@tpoff, %edi
71:         addl %fs:b@tpoff, %edi    # 40 + 2 = 42
72:         movl $60, %eax
73:         syscall
74:         .bss
75:         .align 64
76:     buf:
77:         .zero 8192
78:     ",
79:     );
80:     let exe = dir.join("a.out");
81:     link(&exe, &[o], &[]);
82:     assert_eq!(run(&exe), 42);
83: }
84: 
85: /// A `.tbss` variable with alignment GREATER than `.tdata`'s forces the static
86: /// TLS block base to be over-aligned. peony must align the TLS segment's
87: /// `p_vaddr` to the MAX TLS-section alignment, or the highly-aligned variable
88: /// lands at the wrong address (the bug that crashed large TLS-heavy binaries
89: /// like a self-hosted toolchain — "TLS during destruction" in std). Verified by
90: /// reading both values back through libc + a real `__thread` access.
91: #[test]
92: fn tls_overaligned_block_base() {
93:     let dir = workdir("tls_align");
94:     let Some(exe) = cc_b(
95:         &dir,
96:         "talign",
97:         &[(
98:             "t.c",
99:             "#include <stdio.h>\n\
100:              __thread int small = 5;\n\
101:              __thread _Alignas(128) long big[4] = {11, 22, 33, 44};\n\
102:              int main(void) {\n\
103:                  // Touch both; the over-aligned `big` must be readable/correct.\n\
104:                  if (small != 5) return 1;\n\
105:                  for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) if (big[i] != 11 * (i + 1)) return 2;\n\
106:                  // Its runtime address must satisfy the 128-byte alignment.\n\
107:                  if (((unsigned long)&big[0] & 127) != 0) return 3;\n\
108:                  printf(\"tls-align ok\\n\");\n\
109:                  return 42;\n\
110:              }\n",
111:         )],
112:         false,
113:     ) else {
114:         eprintln!("skipping: toolchain unavailable");
115:         return;
116:     };
117:     // PT_TLS p_vaddr must be aligned to the block's max alignment (>= 128).
118:     let phdrs = readelf(&exe, &["-lW"]);
119:     let tls_line = phdrs
120:         .lines()
121:         .find(|l| l.trim_start().starts_with("TLS"))
122:         .expect("PT_TLS present");
123:     // Columns: TLS off vaddr paddr filesz memsz flg align
124:     let cols: Vec<&str> = tls_line.split_whitespace().collect();
125:     let vaddr = u64::from_str_radix(cols[2].trim_start_matches("0x"), 16).unwrap();
126:     let align = parse_align(cols.last().unwrap());
127:     assert!(align >= 128, "PT_TLS align {align} should be >= 128");
128:     assert_eq!(
129:         vaddr % align,
130:         0,
131:         "PT_TLS vaddr {vaddr:#x} not aligned to {align}"
132:     );
133:     assert_eq!(run(&exe), 42);
134: }
135: 
136: /// Parse a program-header alignment field, which readelf prints as either a hex
137: /// (`0x80`) or decimal value depending on version.
138: fn parse_align(s: &str) -> u64 {
139:     if let Some(hex) = s.strip_prefix("0x") {
140:         u64::from_str_radix(hex, 16).unwrap_or(1)
141:     } else {
142:         s.parse().unwrap_or(1)
143:     }
144: }

1: //! Dynamic-linking tests: link against a real `cc -shared` library, let the
2: //! system `ld.so` resolve the imports at load, and check the program runs.
3: 
4: mod common;
5: use common::*;
6: 
7: /// Import a data symbol from a shared library (resolved via `R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT`).
8: #[test]
9: fn dynamic_data_import() {
10:     let dir = workdir("dyn_data");
11:     compile_shared(&dir, "foo", "int forty_two = 42;\n");
12:     let m = assemble(
13:         &dir,
14:         "m",
15:         ".text\n.globl _start\n_start:\n movq forty_two@GOTPCREL(%rip), %rax\n movl (%rax), %edi\n movl $60,%eax\n syscall\n",
16:     );
17:     let exe = dir.join("a.out");
18:     let dir_str = dir.to_str().unwrap();
19:     link(&exe, &[m], &["-L", dir_str, "-l", "foo"]);
20: 
21:     // It must be a dynamic executable with a NEEDED entry.
22:     let d = readelf(&exe, &["-d"]);
23:     assert!(d.contains("libfoo.so"), "missing DT_NEEDED libfoo.so: {d}");
24:     assert!(d.contains("(RELA)"), "missing DT_RELA: {d}");
25: 
26:     assert_eq!(run_env(&exe, &[("LD_LIBRARY_PATH", dir_str)]), 42);
27: }
28: 
29: /// A direct reference to DSO data uses executable-owned copy storage plus
30: /// `R_X86_64_COPY`, not a GOT `GLOB_DAT` import.
31: #[test]
32: fn dynamic_copy_relocation_for_direct_data_import() {
33:     let dir = workdir("dyn_copy");
34:     compile_shared(&dir, "copy", "int shared_value = 42;\n");
35:     let m = assemble(
36:         &dir,
37:         "m",
38:         ".text\n.globl _start\n_start:\n movl shared_value(%rip), %edi\n movl $60,%eax\n syscall\n",
39:     );
40:     let exe = dir.join("a.out");
41:     let dir_str = dir.to_str().unwrap();
42:     link(&exe, &[m], &["-L", dir_str, "-l", "copy"]);
43: 
44:     let relocs = readelf(&exe, &["-rW"]);
45:     assert!(
46:         relocs.contains("R_X86_64_COPY") && relocs.contains("shared_value"),
47:         "missing copy relocation:\n{relocs}"
48:     );
49:     let syms = readelf(&exe, &["-sW"]);
50:     assert!(
51:         syms.lines().any(|line| {
52:             line.contains("shared_value")
53:                 && line.contains("OBJECT")
54:                 && line.contains("GLOBAL")
55:                 && !line.contains("UND")
56:         }),
57:         "copy symbol should be executable-defined:\n{syms}"
58:     );
59:     assert_eq!(run_env(&exe, &[("LD_LIBRARY_PATH", dir_str)]), 42);
60: }
61: 
62: /// Import a function from a shared library and call it through the GOT.
63: #[test]
64: fn dynamic_function_import() {
65:     let dir = workdir("dyn_func");
66:     compile_shared(&dir, "bar", "int get_answer(void){ return 42; }\n");
67:     let m = assemble(
68:         &dir,
69:         "m",
70:         ".text\n.globl _start\n_start:\n movq get_answer@GOTPCREL(%rip), %rax\n call *%rax\n movl %eax,%edi\n movl $60,%eax\n syscall\n",
71:     );
72:     let exe = dir.join("a.out");
73:     let dir_str = dir.to_str().unwrap();
74:     link(&exe, &[m], &["-L", dir_str, "-l", "bar"]);
75:     assert_eq!(run_env(&exe, &[("LD_LIBRARY_PATH", dir_str)]), 42);
76: }
77: 
78: /// A GNU `ld` linker script (`GROUP(...)`, like the system `libc.so`) is
79: /// expanded to the real shared object it references.
80: #[test]
81: fn linker_script_group() {

83:     compile_shared(&dir, "real", "int forty_two = 42;\n"); // → libreal.so

127: /// Shared objects keep GNU2 TLSDESC accesses and emit loader-filled descriptor

129: #[test]
130: fn shared_object_emits_tlsdesc_relocation() {
131:     let dir = workdir("tlsdesc");
132:     let o = assemble(
133:         &dir,
134:         "tlsdesc",

136:     );

140:     let relocs = readelf(&so, &["-rW"]);

142:         relocs.contains("R_X86_64_TLSDESC"),
143:         "missing TLSDESC dynamic relocation:\n{relocs}"

151: /// Executables relax TLSDESC to local-exec code and do not carry descriptor
152: /// relocations.
153: #[test]
154: fn executable_relaxes_tlsdesc_to_local_exec() {
155:     let dir = workdir("tlsdesc_exe");
156:     let o = assemble(
157:         &dir,
158:         "tlsdesc_exe",
159:         ".text\n.globl _start\n_start:\n leaq tls_first@TLSDESC(%rip), %rax\n call *tls_first@TLSCALL(%rax)\n movl $60,%eax\n syscall\n.section .tdata,\"awT\",@progbits\ntls_first:\n .long 11\ntls_second:\n .long 22\n",
160:     );
161:     let exe = dir.join("a.out");
162:     link(&exe, &[o], &[]);
163: 
164:     let relocs = readelf(&exe, &["-rW"]);
165:     assert!(
166:         !relocs.contains("R_X86_64_TLSDESC"),
167:         "executable should not keep TLSDESC relocations:\n{relocs}"
168:     );
169:     let disasm = objdump(&exe, &["-dr"]);
170:     assert!(
171:         disasm.contains("mov    $0xfffffffffffffff8,%rax") && disasm.contains("xchg   %ax,%ax"),
172:         "TLSDESC sequence was not relaxed like GNU ld:\n{disasm}"
173:     );
174: }
175: 
176: /// A dynamic executable peony produces runs identically to one from GNU `ld`.
177: #[test]
178: fn dynamic_matches_ld() {
179:     let dir = workdir("dyn_diff");
180:     compile_shared(&dir, "v", "int value = 42;\n");
181:     let m = assemble(
182:         &dir,

185:     );

187: 
188:     let peony_exe = dir.join("peony.out");

193:     );
194:     let peony_rc = run_env(&peony_exe, &[("LD_LIBRARY_PATH", dir_str)]);
195: 
196:     // Reference with GNU ld.
197:     let ld_exe = dir.join("ld.out");
198:     let ok = std::process::Command::new("ld")
199:         .args(["-o"])
200:         .arg(&ld_exe)
201:         .args(["-dynamic-linker", "/lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2"])
202:         .arg(&m)
203:         .args(["-L", dir_str, "-lv"])
204:         .status()
205:         .map(|s| s.success())
206:         .unwrap_or(false);
207:

209:     if ok {

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: //! Shared harness for peony's end-to-end tests — the Rust equivalent of mold's
2: //! `test/common.inc`: assemble fixtures with the system toolchain, link them with
3: //! peony, execute, and inspect with `readelf`. Used by every `tests/*.rs` file.
4: #![allow(dead_code)]
5: 
6: use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
7: use std::process::{Command, Output};
8: use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};
9: 
10: pub const PEONY: &str = env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_peony");
11: 
12: static COUNTER: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
13: 
14: /// A fresh, unique temp directory for one test step.
15: pub fn workdir(tag: &str) -> PathBuf {
16:     let n = COUNTER.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
17:     let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("peony-it-{}-{}-{}", std::process::id(), tag, n));
18:     let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
19:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).expect("create workdir");
20:     dir
21: }
22: 
23: /// Assemble x86-64 source → `.o`; returns its path.
24: pub fn assemble(dir: &Path, name: &str, src: &str) -> PathBuf {
25:     assemble_with_args(dir, name, src, &[])
26: }
27: 
28: /// Assemble x86-64 source with extra assembler flags → `.o`; returns its path.
29: pub fn assemble_with_args(dir: &Path, name: &str, src: &str, extra: &[&str]) -> PathBuf {
30:     let s = dir.join(format!("{name}.s"));
31:     let o = dir.join(format!("{name}.o"));
32:     std::fs::write(&s, src).unwrap();
33:     let st = Command::new("as")
34:         .args(extra)
35:         .args(["--64", "-o"])
36:         .arg(&o)
37:         .arg(&s)
38:         .status()
39:         .expect("run `as`");
40:     assert!(st.success(), "assembling {name} failed");
41:     o
42: }
43: 
44: /// Compile C source (freestanding, no PIC) → `.o`.
45: pub fn compile_c(dir: &Path, name: &str, src: &str) -> PathBuf {
46:     let c = dir.join(format!("{name}.c"));
47:     let o = dir.join(format!("{name}.o"));
48:     std::fs::write(&c, src).unwrap();
49:     let st = Command::new("cc")
50:         .args([
51:             "-c",
52:             "-fno-pic",
53:             "-fno-asynchronous-unwind-tables",
54:             "-ffreestanding",
55:             "-o",
56:         ])
57:         .arg(&o)
58:         .arg(&c)
59:         .status()
60:         .expect("run `cc`");
61:     assert!(st.success(), "compiling {name} failed");
62:     o
63: }
64: 
65: /// Build a static archive `name.a` from objects.
66: pub fn archive(dir: &Path, name: &str, objs: &[PathBuf]) -> PathBuf {
67:     let a = dir.join(format!("{name}.a"));
68:     let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&a);
69:     let mut cmd = Command::new("ar");
70:     cmd.arg("rcs").arg(&a);
71:     for o in objs {
72:         cmd.arg(o);
73:     }
74:     assert!(cmd.status().expect("run `ar`").success(), "ar failed");
75:     a
76: }
77: 
78: /// Link with peony; panics with stderr on failure.
79: pub fn link(out: &Path, inputs: &[PathBuf], extra: &[&str]) {
80:     let o = link_raw(out, inputs, extra);
81:     assert!(
82:         o.status.success(),
83:         "peony link failed: {}",
84:         String::from_utf8_lossy(&o.stderr)
85:     );
86: }
87: 
88: /// Link with peony, returning the raw `Output` (for inspecting failure/stderr).
89: pub fn link_raw(out: &Path, inputs: &[PathBuf], extra: &[&str]) -> Output {
90:     let mut cmd = Command::new(PEONY);
91:     cmd.arg("-o").arg(out);
92:     for a in extra {
93:         cmd.arg(a);
94:     }
95:     for i in inputs {
96:         cmd.arg(i);
97:     }
98:     cmd.env("PEONY_LOG", "info").output().expect("run peony")
99: }
100: 
101: /// Reference link with GNU `ld` (for differential checks).
102: pub fn ld_link(out: &Path, inputs: &[PathBuf]) -> bool {
103:     let mut cmd = Command::new("ld");
104:     cmd.arg("-o").arg(out);
105:     for i in inputs {
106:         cmd.arg(i);
107:     }
108:     cmd.status().map(|s| s.success()).unwrap_or(false)
109: }
110: 
111: /// Run an executable and return its exit code.
112: pub fn run(path: &Path) -> i32 {
113:     Command::new(path)
114:         .status()
115:         .expect("run executable")
116:         .code()
117:         .expect("exit code")
118: }
119: 
120: /// Run with extra environment variables (e.g. `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`).
121: pub fn run_env(path: &Path, env: &[(&str, &str)]) -> i32 {
122:     let mut cmd = Command::new(path);
123:     for (k, v) in env {
124:         cmd.env(k, v);
125:     }
126:     cmd.status()
127:         .expect("run executable")
128:         .code()
129:         .expect("exit code")
130: }
131: 
132: /// `cc -print-file-name=<name>` (locate crt objects / libc).
133: pub fn cc_file(name: &str) -> PathBuf {
134:     let out = Command::new("cc")
135:         .arg(format!("-print-file-name={name}"))
136:         .output()
137:         .expect("run cc -print-file-name");
138:     PathBuf::from(String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string())
139: }
140: 
141: /// Compile C source and link a full dynamic executable against the system libc
142: /// using **peony** as the linker (crt1/crti/crtn + `-lc`). Returns the exe path,
143: /// or `None` if the toolchain pieces aren't available.
144: pub fn link_c(dir: &Path, name: &str, src: &str) -> Option<PathBuf> {
145:     let c = dir.join(format!("{name}.c"));
146:     let o = dir.join(format!("{name}.o"));
147:     std::fs::write(&c, src).unwrap();
148:     let st = Command::new("cc")
149:         .args(["-c", "-fno-pic", "-o"])
150:         .arg(&o)
151:         .arg(&c)
152:         .status()
153:         .expect("cc -c");
154:     if !st.success() {
155:         return None;
156:     }
157:     let (crt1, crti, crtn) = (cc_file("crt1.o"), cc_file("crti.o"), cc_file("crtn.o"));
158:     let libcdir = cc_file("libc.so").parent()?.to_path_buf();
159:     if !crt1.exists() {
160:         return None;
161:     }
162:     let exe = dir.join(name);
163:     let out = link_raw(
164:         &exe,
165:         &[crt1, crti, o, crtn],
166:         &["-L", libcdir.to_str().unwrap(), "-l", "c"],
167:     );
168:     if out.status.success() {
169:         Some(exe)
170:     } else {
171:         eprintln!(
172:             "peony link_c failed: {}",
173:             String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
174:         );
175:         None
176:     }
177: }

900: /// `.dynsym` index for an imported TLS symbol (0 if not an import / unknown).
901: fn tls_ref_dynidx(tref: TlsRef, symbols: &SymbolTable) -> u32 {

912: /// Count the TLS dynamic relocations a shared object's TLS GOT will need, to
913: /// size `.rela.dyn` before layout. Each GD pair contributes 1 (local) or 2
914: /// (imported), each TLSDESC pair 1, the LDM pair 1, each IE slot 1.
915: pub fn count_tls_relocs(
916:     objects: &[InputObject],
917:     symbols: &SymbolTable,
918:     tls: &peony_layout::TlsGotInfo,
919: ) -> usize {
920:     let imported = |tref: &TlsRef| -> bool {
921:         match tref {
922:             TlsRef::Local(..) => false,
923:             TlsRef::Global(id) => symbols
924:                 .name_by_id(*id)
925:                 .and_then(|n| symbols.lookup(n))
926:                 .map(|r| r.defined_in.is_none())
927:                 .unwrap_or(true),
928:         }
929:     };
930:     let _ = objects;
931:     let gd: usize = tls.gd.iter().map(|t| if imported(t) { 2 } else { 1 }).sum();
932:     let desc = tls.desc.len();
933:     let ie = tls.ie.len();
934:     let ldm = usize::from(tls.ldm);
935:     gd + desc + ie + ldm
936: }

360: ) -> RelocScanResult {
361:     // Small links scan faster serially: spinning up rayon's global pool for a
362:     // few objects costs more in thread management (`sched_yield`/`futex`) than
363:     // the scan itself. Fan out only once there are enough objects to amortize.
364:     // See the parse-threshold note in main.rs: touching rayon's global pool on a
365:     // small link costs more in futex/sched_yield idle-spin than the scan saves.
366:     const PARALLEL_SCAN_THRESHOLD: usize = 256;
367:     let per_object: Vec<Vec<SyntheticSlot>> = if objects.len() >= PARALLEL_SCAN_THRESHOLD {
368:         objects
369:             .par_iter()
370:             .enumerate()
371:             .map(|(obj_id, obj)| scan_object(obj, obj_id, symbols, shared))
372:             .collect()
373:     } else {
374:         objects
375:             .iter()
376:             .enumerate()
377:             .map(|(obj_id, obj)| scan_object(obj, obj_id, symbols, shared))
378:             .collect()
379:     };
380: 
381:     let mut result = RelocScanResult::new();
382:     for slots in per_object {
383:         for slot in slots {
384:             result.add(slot);
385:         }
386:     }
387:     result
388: }
389: 
390: fn scan_object(
391:     obj: &InputObject,
392:     obj_id: usize,
393:     symbols: &SymbolTable,
394:     shared: bool,
395: ) -> Vec<SyntheticSlot> {
396:     let mut slots = Vec::new();
397:     for sec in &obj.sections {
398:         for reloc in &sec.relocs {
399:             let Some(sym_pos) = obj.symbol_map.get(&reloc.symbol.0).copied() else {
400:                 continue;
401:             };
402:             let Some(sym) = obj.symbols.get(sym_pos) else {
403:                 continue;
404:             };
405:             // A helper to key a TLS reference (locals are file-scoped).
406:             let tls_ref = |sym: &peony_object::InputSymbol| -> TlsRef {
407:                 if sym.binding == Binding::Local {
408:                     TlsRef::Local(obj_id, sym_pos)
409:                 } else {
410:                     match symbols.lookup(&sym.name) {
411:                         Some(r) => TlsRef::Global(r.id),
412:                         None => TlsRef::Local(obj_id, sym_pos),
413:                     }
414:                 }
415:             };
416:             // Initial-Exec (`GOTTPOFF`) ALWAYS needs a GOT slot holding the TP
417:             // offset — in BOTH an executable (filled statically, the offset is
418:             // known) and a shared object (filled at load via R_X86_64_TPOFF64).
419:             // The `mov x@gottpoff(%rip),%reg` access is kept in both cases.
420:             if reloc.r_type == r_x86_64::GOTTPOFF {
421:                 slots.push(SyntheticSlot::TlsIe(tls_ref(sym)));
422:                 continue;
423:             }
424:             // General-/Local-Dynamic: only a shared object keeps them (an
425:             // executable relaxes GD/LD → Local-Exec, needing no GOT slot).
426:             if shared && matches!(reloc.r_type, r_x86_64::TLSGD | r_x86_64::TLSLD) {
427:                 match reloc.r_type {
428:                     r_x86_64::TLSGD => slots.push(SyntheticSlot::TlsGd(tls_ref(sym))),
429:                     r_x86_64::TLSLD => slots.push(SyntheticSlot::TlsLdm),
430:                     _ => unreachable!(),
431:                 }
432:                 continue;
433:             }
434:             if shared && reloc.r_type == r_x86_64::GOTPC32_TLSDESC {
435:                 slots.push(SyntheticSlot::TlsDesc(tls_ref(sym)));
436:                 continue;
437:             }
438: 
439:             // Slots are keyed by global SymbolId; local refs are handled directly.

179: /// Compile + link C source(s) using **peony as `cc`'s linker** (`cc -B<peony>`),
180: /// the way mold's tests drive the toolchain. Returns the exe path, or `None` if
181: /// the toolchain isn't available. `cxx` selects the C++ driver.
182: pub fn cc_b(dir: &Path, name: &str, srcs: &[(&str, &str)], cxx: bool) -> Option<PathBuf> {
183:     cc_b_modeled(dir, name, srcs, cxx, false)
184: }
185: 
186: /// Like [`cc_b`] but links a **PIE** (the default for cc/rustc). The PIE path is
187: /// where copy-relocations, `.gnu.hash` indexing of copy-reloc symbols, and the
188: /// GNU-property/`.tm_clone_table` handling all matter — i.e. the path the C++
189: /// iostream regression tests must exercise.
190: pub fn cc_b_pie(dir: &Path, name: &str, srcs: &[(&str, &str)], cxx: bool) -> Option<PathBuf> {
191:     cc_b_modeled(dir, name, srcs, cxx, true)
192: }
193: 
194: fn cc_b_modeled(
195:     dir: &Path,
196:     name: &str,
197:     srcs: &[(&str, &str)],
198:     cxx: bool,
199:     pie: bool,
200: ) -> Option<PathBuf> {
201:     let bindir = dir.join("ldbin");
202:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&bindir).ok()?;
203:     std::fs::copy(PEONY, bindir.join("ld")).ok()?;
204:     let mut src_paths = Vec::new();
205:     for (fname, body) in srcs {
206:         let p = dir.join(fname);
207:         std::fs::write(&p, body).ok()?;
208:         src_paths.push(p);
209:     }
210:     let exe = dir.join(name);
211:     let driver = if cxx { "c++" } else { "cc" };
212:     let mut cmd = Command::new(driver);
213:     cmd.arg(format!("-B{}/", bindir.display()));
214:     if pie {
215:         cmd.args(["-fpie", "-pie"]);
216:     } else {
217:         cmd.args(["-fno-pie", "-no-pie"]);
218:     }
219:     cmd.arg("-o").arg(&exe);
220:     for p in &src_paths {
221:         cmd.arg(p);
222:     }
223:     match cmd.status() {
224:         Ok(s) if s.success() => Some(exe),
225:         _ => None,
226:     }
227: }
228:

232:     (

240:     let c = dir.join(format!("{name}.c"));
241:     let so = dir.join(format!("lib{name}.so"));

251: }

1160: 
1161: // Notes about General Dynamic and Local Dynamic TLS models below. They may
1162: // require the generation of a pair of GOT entries that have associated dynamic
1163: // relocations. The pair of GOT entries created are of the form GOT[e0] Module
1164: // Index (Used to find pointer to TLS block at run-time) GOT[e1] Offset of
1165: // symbol in TLS block.
1166: //
1167: // Returns the number of relocations processed.
1168: unsigned RelocScan::handleTlsRelocation(RelExpr expr, RelType type,
1169:                                         uint64_t offset, Symbol &sym,
1170:                                         int64_t addend) {
1171:   bool isAArch64 = ctx.arg.emachine == EM_AARCH64;
1172: 
1173:   if (isAArch64)
1174:     if (unsigned processed = handleAArch64PAuthTlsRelocation(
1175:             sec, expr, type, offset, sym, addend))
1176:       return processed;
1177: 
1178:   if (expr == R_TPREL || expr == R_TPREL_NEG)
1179:     return checkTlsLe(offset, sym, type) ? 1 : 0;
1180: 
1181:   bool isRISCV = ctx.arg.emachine == EM_RISCV;
1182: 
1183:   if (oneof<RE_AARCH64_TLSDESC_PAGE, R_TLSDESC, R_TLSDESC_CALL, R_TLSDESC_PC,
1184:             R_TLSDESC_GOTPLT, RE_LOONGARCH_TLSDESC_PAGE_PC>(expr) &&
1185:       ctx.arg.shared) {
1186:     // R_RISCV_TLSDESC_{LOAD_LO12,ADD_LO12_I,CALL} reference a label. Do not
1187:     // set NEEDS_TLSDESC on the label.
1188:     if (expr != R_TLSDESC_CALL) {
1189:       if (isAArch64)
1190:         sym.setFlags(NEEDS_TLSDESC | NEEDS_TLSDESC_NONAUTH);
1191:       else if (!isRISCV || type == R_RISCV_TLSDESC_HI20)
1192:         sym.setFlags(NEEDS_TLSDESC);
1193:       sec->addReloc({expr, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1194:     }
1195:     return 1;
1196:   }
1197: 
1198:   // LoongArch supports IE to LE, DESC GD/LD to IE/LE optimizations in
1199:   // non-extreme code model.
1200:   bool execOptimizeInLoongArch =
1201:       ctx.arg.emachine == EM_LOONGARCH &&
1202:       (type == R_LARCH_TLS_IE_PC_HI20 || type == R_LARCH_TLS_IE_PC_LO12 ||
1203:        type == R_LARCH_TLS_DESC_PC_HI20 || type == R_LARCH_TLS_DESC_PC_LO12 ||
1204:        type == R_LARCH_TLS_DESC_LD || type == R_LARCH_TLS_DESC_CALL ||
1205:        type == R_LARCH_TLS_DESC_PCREL20_S2);
1206: 
1207:   // ARM, Hexagon, LoongArch and RISC-V do not support GD/LD to IE/LE
1208:   // optimizations.
1209:   // RISC-V supports TLSDESC to IE/LE optimizations.
1210:   // For PPC64, if the file has missing R_PPC64_TLSGD/R_PPC64_TLSLD, disable
1211:   // optimization as well.
1212:   bool execOptimize =
1213:       !ctx.arg.shared && ctx.arg.emachine != EM_ARM &&
1214:       ctx.arg.emachine != EM_HEXAGON &&
1215:       (ctx.arg.emachine != EM_LOONGARCH || execOptimizeInLoongArch) &&
1216:       !(isRISCV && expr != R_TLSDESC_PC && expr != R_TLSDESC_CALL) &&
1217:       !sec->file->ppc64DisableTLSRelax;
1218: 
1219:   // If we are producing an executable and the symbol is non-preemptable, it
1220:   // must be defined and the code sequence can be optimized to use
1221:   // Local-Exesec->
1222:   //
1223:   // ARM and RISC-V do not support any relaxations for TLS relocations, however,
1224:   // we can omit the DTPMOD dynamic relocations and resolve them at link time
1225:   // because them are always 1. This may be necessary for static linking as
1226:   // DTPMOD may not be expected at load time.
1227:   bool isLocalInExecutable = !sym.isPreemptible && !ctx.arg.shared;
1228: 
1229:   // Local Dynamic is for access to module local TLS variables, while still
1230:   // being suitable for being dynamically loaded via dlopen. GOT[e0] is the
1231:   // module index, with a special value of 0 for the current module. GOT[e1] is
1232:   // unused. There only needs to be one module index entry.
1233:   if (oneof<R_TLSLD_GOT, R_TLSLD_GOTPLT, R_TLSLD_PC, R_TLSLD_HINT>(expr)) {
1234:     // Local-Dynamic relocs can be optimized to Local-Exesec->
1235:     if (execOptimize) {
1236:       sec->addReloc({ctx.target->adjustTlsExpr(type, R_RELAX_TLS_LD_TO_LE),
1237:                      type, offset, addend, &sym});
1238:       return ctx.target->getTlsGdRelaxSkip(type);
1239:     }
1240:     if (expr == R_TLSLD_HINT)
1241:       return 1;
1242:     ctx.needsTlsLd.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
1243:     sec->addReloc({expr, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1244:     return 1;
1245:   }
1246: 
1247:   // Local-Dynamic relocs can be optimized to Local-Exesec->
1248:   if (expr == R_DTPREL) {
1249:     if (execOptimize)
1250:       expr = ctx.target->adjustTlsExpr(type, R_RELAX_TLS_LD_TO_LE);
1251:     sec->addReloc({expr, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1252:     return 1;
1253:   }
1254: 
1255:   // Local-Dynamic sequence where offset of tls variable relative to dynamic
1256:   // thread pointer is stored in the got. This cannot be optimized to
1257:   // Local-Exesec->
1258:   if (expr == R_TLSLD_GOT_OFF) {
1259:     sym.setFlags(NEEDS_GOT_DTPREL);
1260:     sec->addReloc({expr, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1261:     return 1;
1262:   }
1263: 
1264:   // LoongArch does not support transition from TLSDESC to LE/IE in the extreme
1265:   // code model, in which NEEDS_TLSDESC should set, rather than NEEDS_TLSGD. So
1266:   // we check independently.
1267:   if (ctx.arg.emachine == EM_LOONGARCH &&
1268:       oneof<RE_LOONGARCH_TLSDESC_PAGE_PC, R_TLSDESC, R_TLSDESC_PC,
1269:             R_TLSDESC_CALL>(expr) &&
1270:       !execOptimize) {
1271:     if (expr != R_TLSDESC_CALL) {
1272:       sym.setFlags(NEEDS_TLSDESC);
1273:       sec->addReloc({expr, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1274:     }
1275:     return 1;
1276:   }
1277: 
1278:   if (oneof<RE_AARCH64_TLSDESC_PAGE, R_TLSDESC, R_TLSDESC_CALL, R_TLSDESC_PC,
1279:             R_TLSDESC_GOTPLT, R_TLSGD_GOT, R_TLSGD_GOTPLT, R_TLSGD_PC,
1280:             RE_LOONGARCH_TLSGD_PAGE_PC, RE_LOONGARCH_TLSDESC_PAGE_PC>(expr)) {
1281:     if (!execOptimize) {
1282:       sym.setFlags(NEEDS_TLSGD);
1283:       sec->addReloc({expr, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1284:       return 1;
1285:     }
1286: 
1287:     // Global-Dynamic/TLSDESC can be optimized to Initial-Exec or Local-Exec
1288:     // depending on the symbol being locally defined or not.
1289:     //
1290:     // R_RISCV_TLSDESC_{LOAD_LO12,ADD_LO12_I,CALL} reference a non-preemptible
1291:     // label, so TLSDESC=>IE will be categorized as R_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_LE. We fix
1292:     // the categorization in RISCV::relocateAllosec->
1293:     if (sym.isPreemptible) {
1294:       sym.setFlags(NEEDS_TLSIE);
1295:       sec->addReloc({ctx.target->adjustTlsExpr(type, R_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_IE),
1296:                      type, offset, addend, &sym});
1297:     } else {
1298:       sec->addReloc({ctx.target->adjustTlsExpr(type, R_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_LE),
1299:                      type, offset, addend, &sym});
1300:     }
1301:     return ctx.target->getTlsGdRelaxSkip(type);
1302:   }
1303: 
1304:   if (oneof<R_GOT, R_GOTPLT, R_GOT_PC, RE_AARCH64_GOT_PAGE_PC,
1305:             RE_LOONGARCH_GOT_PAGE_PC, R_GOT_OFF, R_TLSIE_HINT>(expr)) {
1306:     ctx.hasTlsIe.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
1307:     // Initial-Exec relocs can be optimized to Local-Exec if the symbol is
1308:     // locally defined.  This is not supported on SystemZ.
1309:     if (execOptimize && isLocalInExecutable && ctx.arg.emachine != EM_S390) {
1310:       sec->addReloc({R_RELAX_TLS_IE_TO_LE, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1311:     } else if (expr != R_TLSIE_HINT) {
1312:       sym.setFlags(NEEDS_TLSIE);
1313:       // R_GOT needs a relative relocation for PIC on i386 and Hexagon.
1314:       if (expr == R_GOT && ctx.arg.isPic &&
1315:           !ctx.target->usesOnlyLowPageBits(type))
1316:         addRelativeReloc<true>(ctx, *sec, offset, sym, addend, expr, type);
1317:       else
1318:         sec->addReloc({expr, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1319:     }
1320:     return 1;
1321:   }
1322: 
1323:   // LoongArch TLS GD/LD relocs reuse the RE_LOONGARCH_GOT, in which
1324:   // NEEDS_TLSIE shouldn't set. So we check independently.
1325:   if (ctx.arg.emachine == EM_LOONGARCH && expr == RE_LOONGARCH_GOT &&
1326:       execOptimize && isLocalInExecutable) {
1327:     ctx.hasTlsIe.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
1328:     sec->addReloc({R_RELAX_TLS_IE_TO_LE, type, offset, addend, &sym});
1329:     return 1;

760:   // (TP + gap + padding) is congruent to p_vaddr modulo p_align.
761:   //
762:   // Variant 2. Static TLS blocks, followed by alignment padding are placed
763:   // before TP. The alignment padding is added so that (TP - padding -
764:   // p_memsz) is congruent to p_vaddr modulo p_align.
765:   PhdrEntry *tls = ctx.tlsPhdr;
766:   if (!tls) // Reported an error in getSymVA
767:     return 0;
768:   switch (ctx.arg.emachine) {
769:     // Variant 1.
770:   case EM_ARM:
771:   case EM_AARCH64:
772:     return s.getVA(ctx, 0) + ctx.arg.wordsize * 2 +
773:            ((tls->p_vaddr - ctx.arg.wordsize * 2) & (tls->p_align - 1));
774:   case EM_MIPS:
775:   case EM_PPC:
776:   case EM_PPC64:
777:     // Adjusted Variant 1. TP is placed with a displacement of 0x7000, which is
778:     // to allow a signed 16-bit offset to reach 0x1000 of TCB/thread-library
779:     // data and 0xf000 of the program's TLS segment.
780:     return s.getVA(ctx, 0) + (tls->p_vaddr & (tls->p_align - 1)) - 0x7000;
781:   case EM_LOONGARCH:
782:   case EM_RISCV:
783:     // See the comment in handleTlsRelocation. For TLSDESC=>IE,
784:     // R_RISCV_TLSDESC_{LOAD_LO12,ADD_LO12_I,CALL} also reach here. While
785:     // `tls` may be null, the return value is ignored.
786:     if (s.type != STT_TLS)
787:       return 0;
788:     return s.getVA(ctx, 0) + (tls->p_vaddr & (tls->p_align - 1));
789: 
790:     // Variant 2.
791:   case EM_HEXAGON:
792:   case EM_S390:
793:   case EM_SPARCV9:
794:   case EM_386:
795:   case EM_X86_64:
796:     return s.getVA(ctx, 0) - tls->p_memsz -
797:            ((-tls->p_vaddr - tls->p_memsz) & (tls->p_align - 1));
798:   default:
799:     llvm_unreachable("unhandled ctx.arg.emachine");
800:   }
801: }
802: 
803: uint64_t InputSectionBase::getRelocTargetVA(Ctx &ctx, const Relocation &r,
804:                                             uint64_t p) const {
805:   int64_t a = r.addend;
806:   switch (r.expr) {
807:   case R_ABS:
808:   case R_DTPREL:
809:   case R_RELAX_TLS_LD_TO_LE_ABS:
810:   case R_RELAX_GOT_PC_NOPIC:
811:   case RE_AARCH64_AUTH:
812:   case RE_RISCV_ADD:
813:   case RE_RISCV_LEB128:
814:     return r.sym->getVA(ctx, a);
815:   case R_ADDEND:
816:     return a;
817:   case R_ADDEND_NEG:
818:     return -static_cast<uint64_t>(a);
819:   case R_RELAX_HINT:
820:     return 0;
821:   case RE_ARM_SBREL:
822:     return r.sym->getVA(ctx, a) - getARMStaticBase(*r.sym);
823:   case R_GOT:
824:   case RE_AARCH64_AUTH_GOT:
825:   case R_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_IE_ABS:
826:     return r.sym->getGotVA(ctx) + a;
827:   case RE_LOONGARCH_GOT:
828:     // The LoongArch TLS GD relocs reuse the R_LARCH_GOT_PC_LO12 reloc r.type
829:     // for their page offsets. The arithmetics are different in the TLS case
830:     // so we have to duplicate some logic here.
831:     if (r.sym->hasFlag(NEEDS_TLSGD) && r.type != R_LARCH_TLS_IE_PC_LO12)
832:       // Like RE_LOONGARCH_TLSGD_PAGE_PC but taking the absolute value.
833:       return ctx.in.got->getGlobalDynAddr(*r.sym) + a;
834:     return r.sym->getGotVA(ctx) + a;
835:   case R_GOTONLY_PC:
836:     return ctx.in.got->getVA() + a - p;
837:   case R_GOTPLTONLY_PC:
838:     return ctx.in.gotPlt->getVA() + a - p;
839:   case R_GOTREL:
840:   case RE_PPC64_RELAX_TOC:
841:     return r.sym->getVA(ctx, a) - ctx.in.got->getVA();
842:   case R_GOTPLTREL:
843:     return r.sym->getVA(ctx, a) - ctx.in.gotPlt->getVA();
844:   case R_GOTPLT:
845:   case R_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_IE_GOTPLT:
846:     return r.sym->getGotVA(ctx) + a - ctx.in.gotPlt->getVA();
847:   case R_TLSLD_GOT_OFF:
848:   case R_GOT_OFF:
849:   case R_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_IE_GOT_OFF:
850:     return r.sym->getGotOffset(ctx) + a;
851:   case RE_AARCH64_GOT_PAGE_PC:
852:   case RE_AARCH64_AUTH_GOT_PAGE_PC:
853:   case RE_AARCH64_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_IE_PAGE_PC:
854:     return getAArch64Page(r.sym->getGotVA(ctx) + a) - getAArch64Page(p);
855:   case RE_AARCH64_GOT_PAGE:
856:     return r.sym->getGotVA(ctx) + a - getAArch64Page(ctx.in.got->getVA());
857:   case R_GOT_PC:
858:   case RE_AARCH64_AUTH_GOT_PC:
859:   case R_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_IE:
860:     return r.sym->getGotVA(ctx) + a - p;
861:   case R_GOTPLT_GOTREL:
862:     return r.sym->getGotPltVA(ctx) + a - ctx.in.got->getVA();
863:   case R_GOTPLT_PC:
864:     return r.sym->getGotPltVA(ctx) + a - p;
865:   case RE_LOONGARCH_GOT_PAGE_PC:
866:   case RE_LOONGARCH_RELAX_TLS_GD_TO_IE_PAGE_PC:
867:     if (r.sym->hasFlag(NEEDS_TLSGD))
868:       return getLoongArchPageDelta(ctx.in.got->getGlobalDynAddr(*r.sym) + a, p,
869:                                    r.type);
870:     return getLoongArchPageDelta(r.sym->getGotVA(ctx) + a, p, r.type);
871:   case RE_MIPS_GOTREL:
872:     return r.sym->getVA(ctx, a) - ctx.in.mipsGot->getGp(file);
873:   case RE_MIPS_GOT_GP:
874:     return ctx.in.mipsGot->getGp(file) + a;
875:   case RE_MIPS_GOT_GP_PC: {
876:     // R_MIPS_LO16 expression has RE_MIPS_GOT_GP_PC r.type iif the target
877:     // is _gp_disp symbol. In that case we should use the following
878:     // formula for calculation "AHL + GP - P + 4". For details see p. 4-19 at
879:     // ftp://www.linux-mips.org/pub/linux/mips/doc/ABI/mipsabi.pdf
